races.race_name: Chesham Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Aperoll
runners.spotlight: Well-backed 9-4 favourite when beating her 11 rivals on debut in a Newbury fillies' maiden (6.5f, good to firm) this month; this race will be tougher, but her trainer was very complimentary about her after that win and fillies have won two of the last three runnings.
runners.comment: Did it well on her Newbury debut; trainer was very complimentary about her afterwards

runners.name: Aix La Chapelle
runners.spotlight: Superbly bred colt who was very much the stable second string according to the market (and deserted by Ryan Moore) for his debut in a Curragh maiden (7f, good to yielding) 15 days ago but he managed to overcome greenness to beat his five rivals, ultimately a shade cosily; likely to have learnt plenty from that and a major player for yard that has won this race seven times.
runners.comment: Overcame greenness to make a winning debut at the Curragh; stable has won this seven times

runners.name: Time For The Moon
runners.spotlight: Could manage only third of four when 11-10 for Haydock debut (7f, good to soft) last month, but he managed to come home just over 7l clear in a restricted novice at Musselburgh (7f, good) 13 days ago, despite rolling around in front; this race is tougher, but he looks a nice type and is respected.
runners.comment: Won by just over 7l at Musselburgh on his second start; looks a nice type; respected

runners.name: Sea Venture
runners.spotlight: Impressive when coming home 3l clear on her debut in a Haydock fillies' maiden (6f, good to soft; 8-1) last month, especially as she had to overcome all sorts of traffic issues; open to progress and the extra furlong won't be a problem, but the form of the Haydock race isn't really working out, so improvement will be required.
runners.comment: Impressive when winning on her Haydock debut but the form isn't really working out

runners.name: Revels
runners.spotlight: 150,000gns foal, 350,000gns yearling; 8-11 for last month's debut in a Redcar maiden (7f, good) and, despite showing signs of greenness, he came home 3l clear of his nearest rival (third has won since); will have learnt plenty from that and is respected for stable that took this race in 2022.
runners.comment: Overcame greenness when winning on Redcar debut; will have learnt plenty from that

runners.name: Nola Soul
runners.spotlight: $220,000 yearling; overcame both taking a strong hold and greenness when staying on well to make a winning debut in a maiden at Leopardstown (7f, good) last month (third has won since); very likely to have learnt from that and he's one to be interested in.
runners.comment: Green when making a winning debut at Leopardstown; open to improvement; interesting

runners.name: Bayside
runners.spotlight: Improved from her debut when winning a fillies' restricted novice at Beverley (5f, good to soft) in April, but was held under her penalty in a fillies' novice at Nottingham (6f, good) last month; would be a surprise winner.
runners.comment: Won at Beverley in April, but held at Nottingham last time; would be a surprise winner
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Civ & B R Millman

runners.name: Sword Salute
runners.spotlight: Could hardly have been more impressive when justifying favouritism in a Lingfield restricted maiden (7f, good to firm) 19 days ago, but he had previously finished over 4l behind Revels when third of seven on Redcar debut (7f, good); even with the subsequent progress, that's quite a margin to turn around, especially with that rival open to improvement.
runners.comment: Impressive at Lingfield last time, but finished over 4l behind Revels the time before
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Celtic Charioteer
runners.spotlight: Improved from his debut and appreciated the longer trip when justifying favouritism in a Leicester maiden (7f, good) ten days ago; should be winning more races, but unlikely to be this one.
runners.comment: Won at Leicester ten days ago on second start; unlikely to follow up in this

runners.name: On Just Terms
runners.spotlight: $100,000 yearling, 220,000gns breeze-up 2yo; half-brother to three winners including Countessa (11.6f turf/1m4f AW; RPR 79) and Junipermarshmallow (US 7.5f/8.5f turf 2yo including stakes); dam 7f AW winner (80), closely related to multiple 1m Group 1 winner Alpha Centauri; this is a tough race in which to make his debut, but he must be well regarded by his top stable and this race did go to a newcomer four years go; market should be revealing.
runners.comment: This is a tough race in which to make his debut but he must be well regarded by top stable

runners.name: Sydney Carton
runners.spotlight: 5-1 and did all his best work late on when third of eight on debut in a Beverley novice (7.5f, good to firm; hooded, but not today) last month; open to improvement, but the form looks ordinary and this is likely to prove much too hot.
runners.comment: Kept on for third on his Beverley debut, but this is likely to prove much too hot

runners.name: Whispering Moon
runners.spotlight: 100,000gns yearling; caught the eye when staying on strongly to finish third of ten on his debut in a Thirsk novice (7f, good; 18-1) last month; that was a promising start, but he still finished about 5l behind Pikachu and is unlikely to be up to this task.
runners.comment: Promising third at Thirsk but finished about 5l behind Pikachu; unlikely to be up to this
runners.owner: Mr And Mrs J D Cotton

runners.name: Pikachu
runners.spotlight: 450,000gns yearling; 4-1 when beaten half a length into second of ten (well clear of the third) on last month's debut in a Thirsk novice (7f, good); should be winning races in due course, but will need a good deal more if he's to go one better in this; stable triple-handed.
runners.comment: Beaten half a length on his Thirsk debut, but will need a good deal more at this level

runners.name: South Dakota
runners.spotlight: Looked the stable's third string (and was apprentice ridden) when fourth of eight in a Curragh maiden on debut (6f, good) last month; the form has been well advertised by the wins of the second and third and the extra furlong should suit, but improvement will be needed.
runners.comment: Fourth on his Curragh debut and form working out well, but improvement will be needed
runners.owner: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg

runners.name: Romanza
runners.spotlight: 160,000gns yearling; 8-1 when fourth of ten on his debut in a novice here (6f, good) last month; subsequent wins by the first and fifth give the form some substance and the extra furlong should suit, but he needs to have improved considerably.
runners.comment: Fourth on his debut over 6f here last month; needs to have improved considerably

races.race_name: King George V Stakes (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Al Azd
runners.spotlight: Blinkered on last four starts; asserted late on in a 1m3f maiden at Southwell (AW) in April and stormed clear in seven-runner handicap at Doncaster (1m4f, good to firm) two weeks later; only sixth of ten in the London Gold Cup at Newbury in May but he stayed on from last place and that was over just 1m2f; this return to 1m4f looks a far better fit.
runners.comment: Stayed on from last in the 1m2f London Gold Cup; return to 1m4f looks a far better fit

runners.name: Into The Light
runners.spotlight: Won debut at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) in February; outgunned by yesterday's Queen's Vase fifth Ravenspire at Southwell (1m4f, AW) in March but found his stride a little too late when pipped in a handicap at Sandown (1m2f, good; winner took the London Gold Cup next time) in April, so a return to 1m4f looks very much in order; up 5lb but strongly considered.
runners.comment: Return to 1m4f looks very much in order after Sandown 2nd; up 5lb but strongly considered

runners.name: Galilean Quality
runners.spotlight: Pontefract 1m2f winner (soft) as 2yo; contested three 1m4f handicaps this season and came a creditable third at Chester (good) in between winning twice in small fields on AW; latest win was despite veering badly right, away from the whip; up 4lb and, although he may have more in the tank, this race looks so much harder.
runners.comment: 2-3 over 1m4f this season; may have more in the tank but this race looks so much harder

runners.name: Amora Queen
runners.spotlight: 2-3 as 2yo and best form when second on handicap/seasonal debut at Doncaster (1m2f, good to firm) in April; however, the heavy defeats when finishing last in the Lingfield Oaks Trial and a Goodwood handicap, both at about 1m4f, seem to be powerful negatives.
runners.comment: Heavy defeats last 2 starts at 1m4f in the Lingfield Oaks Trial and a Goodwood handicap

runners.name: Tierra Del Toro
runners.spotlight: Dam is sister to dual Arc winner Treve; 8-1, good third of ten in the London Gold Cup at Newbury (1m2f, good; unraced on firmer) on his seasonal/handicap debut; raised 2lb but there's a big chance he can raise his game again with that run under his belt and this extra 2f.
runners.comment: Third in the London Gold Cup at Newbury (1m2f, good) on return; can raise his game again

runners.name: Waterford Castle
runners.spotlight: Maiden after seven races, runner-up in all four since joining George Boughey; three of those were handicaps (8.6f/1m2f) so he should be far more exposed than most of today's rivals, but he has been improving (wore cheekpieces last time) and 1m4f now looks worth a go.
runners.comment: 0-7 but second in all four with this yard; improving and 1m4f now looks worth a go

runners.name: Joulany
runners.spotlight: 800,000gns Sea The Stars yearling, brother to multiple Group 3 winner Al Aasy (1m2f-1m5f); turned over at odds-on in three of his four starts but brings some serious form to today's handicap debut, having been third in blanket finish to a strong novice at Newbury (1m2f, good; hooded) on reappearance and second to Galiyan, who was sixth when favourite for yesterday's Queen's Vase, in maiden at Chester (1m4f, good); gelded since and now tongue tied.
runners.comment: Turned over at odds-on in three of four runs but some serious form; gelded and tongue tied
runners.owner: Al Shaqab Racing, Amo Racing & Valmont

runners.name: Genchev
runners.spotlight: Started his career in a Listed race but it wasn't until his fifth start that he got off the mark, showing improved form with a switch to front-running in a maiden at Navan (1m2f, yielding); saw off a serious challenge that day and there's enough stamina in the family for 1m4f to be interesting.
runners.comment: Improved form with a switch to front-running in 1m2f maiden at Navan; 1m4f is interesting
runners.owner: David Spratt & Sean Jones & Mrs Lynne Lyons

runners.name: Dial Me In
runners.spotlight: Perhaps unsuited by soft ground and/or front-running on second start, as he's otherwise improved his rating every time; tongue tied for minor honours in two median auction races (1m and 10.5f) this term and, despite his pedigree, 1m4f for handicap debut looks interesting on the latest evidence.
runners.comment: Despite his pedigree, 1m4f for handicap debut looks interesting on the latest evidence

runners.name: Atomic City
runners.spotlight: All three races were Irish maidens in the mud, 1-3 when winning on the nod at Cork (10.5f) in early May; ground it out from the front that day; now tongue tied first time; makes handicap debut, for trainer who runs four.
runners.comment: Ground it out from front for 10.5f maiden win in the mud; tongue tied for handicap debut

runners.name: Believed
runners.spotlight: Frankel half-sister to four winners, notably 2022 Melbourne Cup hero Gold Trip; improver in her three Irish maidens (AW/good) and wore a hood in last two; latest was an easy win by nearly 9l when upped to 1m4f at Dundalk; handicap newcomer who should have more to offer.
runners.comment: Improver in her Irish maidens, easy win by nearly 9l latest when upped to 1m4f at Dundalk

runners.name: Enceladus
runners.spotlight: Third in his two maidens as 2yo, before winning one against 15 rivals in a good duel at Cork (10.5f, good to yielding) in April; runner-up took same position behind Cannes three weeks later; Sea The Stars colt who looks scopey and open to plenty of improvement, including at 1m4f; one of four handicap debutants in this for his yard; very much one to note.
runners.comment: 10.5f maiden winner; looks scopey and open to plenty of improvement, including at 1m4f

runners.name: Arc Ole Ole
runners.spotlight: Unraced on firmer than good; scored at Bath (1m, soft) last October on second start, narrowly obliged in handicap at Doncaster (1m2f, good to soft) in March and went in again at York (1m4f, good) five weeks ago; travelled smoothly and asserted gamely in 14-runner handicap the last time, which has seen him raised 10lb since but he's probably not reached his ceiling yet.
runners.comment: On a four-timer; up 10lb since York (1m4f) but he's probably not reached his ceiling yet

runners.name: Birgham Dub
runners.spotlight: Got up close home at Haydock (7f, good) last July in his only 2yo start and reappeared with a clearcut novice win at Southwell (1m4f, AW) in April; never able to challenge in a steadily run French Listed race (1m4f, very soft) five weeks ago, coming fifth of eight but earning his highest rating; could easily remain capable of better but it is needed.
runners.comment: 2-2 in Britain before never able to challenge in a steadily run, 1m4f, French Listed race

runners.name: Heyzoom
runners.spotlight: Favourite on all three starts, placed twice before winning 11-runner maiden at Newbury (1m2f, good) five weeks ago and it looked as if 1m4f would help; runner-up has been a wide-margin winner since; from a famous family and should bring potential to this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Favourite all three starts, winning latest; from famous family and should bring potential

runners.name: Cannes
runners.spotlight: 500,000euros yearling by Sea The Stars; runner-up to notable rivals on testing ground on first two starts; 4-5 when going one better in an eight-runner maiden at Leopardstown (1m4f, good) last month, leading 2f out and gradually asserting to finish well on top; that race has produced winners since and this colt has big-race entries; promising.
runners.comment: Twice 2nd before gradually asserting in a Leopardstown maiden (1m4f, good); yard runs four
runners.owner: Al Shaqab Racing & Stall Perlen AB

runners.name: Blue Hercules
runners.spotlight: Second start was vastly better than his debut and he improved again when front-running second of eight in a novice at Chepstow (1m2f, good to firm) three weeks ago; the winner runs in Friday's Group 2 King Edward VII, albeit at likely big odds; dark horse who is warming to his task and may well stay 1m4f, but this handicap debut comes in a fiercely competitive race.
runners.comment: Warming to his task but this handicap debut comes in a fiercely competitive race

runners.name: Golden Knight
runners.spotlight: Made all in a novice at Newmarket (1m, good) in October on second start; beaten 13l when sixth of  eight in Listed race at Sandown (1m, good) three weeks ago but he's by a good stamina influence in Camelot and this is a major step up in trip for handicap debut.
runners.comment: By a good stamina influence and steps up from 1m for this handicap debut

runners.name: Guildmaster
runners.spotlight: Justified favouritism at Lingfield (1m, AW) in December on debut; he could not lay a glove on the principals this term in a novice at Newmarket or the Group 2 Dante at York, both over 1m2f on good ground, and his seventh of eight when 28-1 in the latter seems to have lumbered him with a stiff opening mark today; 1m4f will probably suit him (backed up by two smart brothers) and he is also blinkered first time, but others have more solid claims.
runners.comment: Stiff mark after 7th of eight in Group 2 Dante but 1m4f will probably suit; blinkers now

races.race_name: Ribblesdale Stakes (Group 2) (Fillies)

runners.name: Gilded Prize
runners.spotlight: Made it 3-3, again with plenty in hand, when making all in the Group 3 Prix Cleopatre at Saint-Cloud in April (10.5f, good; Warriors Whisper second); disappointing favourite in the Group 2 Prix Saint-Alary at Longchamp (1m2f, soft) since and Colin Keane, who has been on board for her last three starts, partners fellow Juddmonte filly Legacy Link today; however, this French raider could play a leading role if tapping back into her previous promise.
runners.comment: Disappointing at Longchamp but 3-3 previously and could play leading role if back on song

runners.name: Legacy Link
runners.spotlight: Built upon her abundant 2yo promise to win the Group 3 Musidora Stakes at York (10.2f, good) last month and she was a clear second in the Oaks at Epsom (1m4f, good to soft) 13 days ago, albeit no match for the winner; she had a hard race last time and there is a worry that this assignment may come too soon, but she sets a strong form standard for the rest to aim at.
runners.comment: Oaks runner-up; that was just 13 days ago but strong claims if at the top of her game

runners.name: Earth Shot
runners.spotlight: 5l win at Newmarket (1m2f, good) in May on second start and then went very close in Listed race at Goodwood (1m2f, good), behind Inis Mor who was subsequently third in the French Oaks; dam was a 1m6f Group 3 winner and the step up in trip could be a good move; one to consider.
runners.comment: Went very close in Listed race at Goodwood and the form has been franked; one to consider

runners.name: Composing
runners.spotlight: Won Group 3 (7.2f, good to yielding) and Group 2 (7f, good) races in Ireland last summer; she's raced five times subsequently and hasn't kicked on but she's the sole representative of Aidan O'Brien, who has won the last three runnings, and perhaps first-time blinkers will trigger a revival.
runners.comment: Hasn't kicked on from last summer's Group-race wins; perhaps blinkers will trigger revival

runners.name: Brilliant Star
runners.spotlight: Impressive from the front in novice races at Yarmouth (11.4f, good to firm) and Kempton (1m3f, AW) this spring; soundly beaten when 5-2 for Listed race at Goodwood (1m2f, good) last month and has plenty to prove in an even tougher contest today, but she retains potential and her dam won this race for connections in 2019 off the back of a Listed defeat; not written off.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in Listed race at Goodwood but impressive previously and not written off

runners.name: Venetia
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; won on handicap debut here (1m2f, good to firm) in May before handicap second at Goodwood (1m4f, good), and she went one better in a Listed race there (1m2f, soft) 11 days ago in first-time cheekpieces (retained); progressive but further improvement is needed in this much hotter race.
runners.comment: Listed win at Goodwood recently but further improvement is needed in this much hotter race

runners.name: Warriors Whisper
runners.spotlight: 1,000,000euros yearling who won on debut at Deauville (9.5f, AW) in February before Listed third at Saint-Cloud (1m2f, good to soft); no match for the winner Gilded Prize when runner-up in Group 3 at Saint-Cloud (10.5f, good) last time and she's up against it on the face of it, but there should be a stronger gallop today which could help this headstrong filly settle better; not ruled out each-way.
runners.comment: Up against it on the face of it but a stronger gallop could help; not ruled out each-way

runners.name: Johanna Walsh
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the third attempt when readily making all in a maiden at Leopardstown (1m2f, good) last month; others have achieved considerably more but she's on the up and her trainer is also responsible for impressive Oaks winner Thundering On; there can be optimism that this Sea The Stars filly will be suited by the step up in trip and she's well worth a second look.
runners.comment: Won Leopardstown maiden; trainer also responsible for impressive Oaks winner Thundering On

runners.name: Lady Roisia
runners.spotlight: Won at Nottingham (8.3f, good to soft) last October on her second run and a respectable third of six in Listed race at Newbury (1m2f, good) on reappearance; her dam won the Oaks for the yard and she's open to further improvement, but it's very necessary in today's company; one of three for stable.
runners.comment: Respectable Listed third on reappearance; open to improvement but it's very necessary

runners.name: Golden Orbit
runners.spotlight: Won at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) last September on sole 2yo start; well-beaten fourth of six on comeback in Listed race at Newbury (1m2f, good) last month; she still looked a work in progress last time and her best days lie ahead, but a sizeable step forward is needed in first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Very lightly raced and her best days lie ahead, but a sizeable step forward is needed
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

runners.name: Dark Lucinda
runners.spotlight: Wore this hood on last month's debut at Gowran Park (9.4f, good to yielding) and won in fine style, making most; this is a vastly tougher test and others have far more substance to their form, but she's unexposed and with a respected stable; a market move would be interesting.
runners.comment: Won on last month's debut at Gowran Park; this is vastly tougher but she's unexposed
runners.owner: Robert J W Moran & Ballylinch Stud

runners.name: Maldives
runners.spotlight: 2,900,000gns yearling; runner-up on debut at Yarmouth (1m2f, good) in April before well-beaten last of six in Listed race at Newmarket (1m2f, good to firm); can win races but it will be a major shock if she lands this one.
runners.comment: 2,900,000gns yearling; has shown ability but dramatic improvement is needed today

races.race_name: Gold Cup (Group 1)

runners.name: Scandinavia
runners.spotlight: Plenty went wrong for him in the Queen's Vase here one year ago (still a close fifth) but unbeaten with cheekpieces added for his five starts since; that includes the St Leger at Doncaster (by a neck from Rahiebb) in September and two 1m6f events this spring (familiar route to this race for his stable) but also, and perhaps most tellingly, the 2m Goodwood Cup last July in which he asserted late on from last year's Gold Cup second; high class and there's plenty in those 1m6f/2m performances which suggests he will be a thorough stayer.
runners.comment: High class at 1m6f/2m and plenty about him suggests he will be a thorough stayer
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Sweet William
runners.spotlight: Third and fourth in the last two renewals of this race and his 19l defeat last year was the only time in his career that he's finished out of the top three; being ridden closer to the pace that day seemingly backfired, as he's usually smuggled into each-way contention in the closing stages; beat Caballo De Mar (who gave 2lb) in one tight finish this year and not quite able to reel in Dubai Future in another; probably as good as ever but winning the Gold Cup may again prove a bit beyond him.
runners.comment: Third and fourth in the last two renewals of this race and he's extremely reliable

runners.name: Trawlerman
runners.spotlight: High class, a thorough stayer and a right battler, the powerful combination of which were seen to prime effect here as a 7yo last year when he made the running (he doesn't have to) to win the Gold Cup on good to firm by 7l, having been a close second the year before; just as good when he won here on final start last term; it's seemingly been touch and go whether he defends his crown, as he has been denied a preliminary run this time round and has developed a rare eye problem which makes him sensitive to sunlight, but he's the one to beat if judged on those sterling performances of 2025.
runners.comment: Has seemingly experienced a troubled build-up this year but he won this race in 2025 by 7l

runners.name: Rahiebb
runners.spotlight: Two clashes with Scandinavia last term (Royal Ascot and the St Leger) indicated there was little between them at about 1m6f; progress from 3yo to 4yo has delighted his trainer, backed up with an authoritative win from Al Nayyir in the Yorkshire Cup at York, again over 1m6f; lots to like but the difficulty is knowing how well he will be suited by 2m4f.
runners.comment: Little between him and Scandinavia as 3yo; authoritative return over 1m6f but this is 2m4f

runners.name: Caballo De Mar
runners.spotlight: Huge improver last term, including second in the 1m6f handicap at this meeting and winning two Group events in the autumn, notably the Group 1 Cadran at Longchamp (2m4f, very soft); every bit as good this season, as he gave Sweet William 2lb when short-headed here (2m, good to firm) and followed that with a narrow Group 1 win at Longchamp; today's company demands a bit more but the thorough test of stamina will suit and he's one to take seriously.
runners.comment: Today's company demands a bit more but he's a 2m4f Group 1 winner; one to take seriously

runners.name: Al Riffa
runners.spotlight: Second at last year's Royal Ascot in the 1m4f Hardwicke and 4l winner of the 1m6f Irish St Leger; ran on strongly from out the back when seventh in the Melbourne Cup (2m) in November and ended up third to Caballo De Mar in blanket finish to a Longchamp Group 1 (nearly 2m) four weeks ago, having been last of nine 2f out; those do not mean he's an assured stayer on this move up to 2m4f but he brings among the best form and has to enter calculations.
runners.comment: Not an assured stayer on this move up to 2m4f but he brings among the best form

runners.name: Miss Alpilles
runners.spotlight: Suited by step up to 2m when winning a Listed event at Newmarket (good) on final start last term; 40-1 and perhaps in need of the race when she ran out of steam behind Sweet William and Caballo De Mar here (2m; beaten 8l) on reappearance, and staying-on second at York (1m6f) next time was better, but there is nothing in her form which suggests she is up to today's task.
runners.comment: Stays 2m well but there is nothing in her form which suggests she is up to today's task

runners.name: Al Nayyir
runners.spotlight: While his form since has not quite reached the heights of 2024, he's still been pretty reliable and got back on the scoresheet at Meydan (1m6f Group 3) in February; however, he twice found Trawlerman far too strong for him in Britain last season and couldn't match Rahiebb late on in last month's Yorkshire Cup; unraced beyond 2m but it's hard to envisage the new trip working sufficiently in his favour.
runners.comment: Unraced beyond 2m but it's hard to envisage this 2m4f working sufficiently in his favour

runners.name: Carmers
runners.spotlight: Won last May/June in his first three races, notably the 1m6f Queen's Vase at this meeting (good to firm) where he seized the initiative entering the straight and finished with three of today's rivals snapping at his heels; Scandinavia and Rahiebb comfortably turned the tables on him in the St Leger at Doncaster in September, however; latest start may have bolstered hopes, as he stepped up to 2m and switched from blinkers to a visor when well on top in a Listed race, but that was in far less demanding company.
runners.comment: Beat three of these over 1m6f here last year; first win since was last time in 2m Listed

runners.name: Furthur
runners.spotlight: Second to Carmers in bunched finish to 1m6f Queen's Vase here last year and impressive in Group 3 at Newbury (1m5f, again good to firm) last August; he's been put firmly in his place on all four outings since, however, beaten just over 4l behind Scandinavia and Rahiebb in the St Leger at Doncaster on first occasion, which was creditable, but singularly underwhelming this season in Yorkshire Cup and 2m Sandown Group 3; needs a huge turnaround.
runners.comment: Just over 4l behind Scandinavia and Rahiebb in the St Leger and underwhelming this season

runners.name: Dubai Future
runners.spotlight: 10yo who has generally fared much better in the Middle East than in Britain but showed far too much toe for the others when stealing a march on Sweet William in 2m Group 3 at Sandown on latest start (hung on by a neck); never remotely near the principals when 14l third in this last year and he's probably not the out-and-out stayer that's required to win it.
runners.comment: 14l third last year and he's probably not the out-and-out stayer that's required to win it

races.race_name: Britannia Stakes (Heritage Handicap) (Colts & Geldings)

runners.name: We're Goosers
runners.spotlight: Yet to run a bad race as the sixth at Newmarket (1m, good) on handicap debut was a close one; back over 7f there and sent off the 4-11 favourite, he romped home by the thick end of 10l to earn today's 11lb higher mark; that rise asks serious questions, but he has the wind in his sails and Oisin Murphy would have had options for this.
runners.comment: Hiked up 11lb for winning so well at Newmarket; Oisin Murphy had other options in this

runners.name: St Anton
runners.spotlight: Reliable and progressive, but he's 6lb higher than when narrowly winning a Class 4 handicap at Carlisle 19 days ago and now 9lb higher than when his season began; he may have revealed his hand.
runners.comment: He's 6lb higher than when narrowly winning a Class 4 handicap at Carlisle 19 days ago

runners.name: Charly
runners.spotlight: Multiple wins in France and fairly scooted clear for a wide-margin success at Saint-Cloud last month (1m); however, that was on soft ground that seems to suit him well and whether he'll be so efficient on this surface remains to be seen (has won on good); ability respected, however.
runners.comment: Very impressive last time at Saint-Cloud but soft ground seems to suit him very well
runners.owner: Ecurie HDBB, Mme Susan Harris & C Bloodstock

runners.name: Tales Of Wisdom
runners.spotlight: Sole defeat was at Kempton (behind Langstone) where he had a bad draw and tried a hood, which was soon dispensed with when pushed out to beat a subsequent winner with the pair clear at Newmarket a month ago (1m, good); brings potential and lots of it into his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Excuses for sole defeat; brings potential and lots of it into his handicap debut

runners.name: Crest Of Fire
runners.spotlight: 0-5 as a 2yo but there was plenty of encouragement, including a 2l defeat here behind Laureate Crown; gelded prior to this campaign which he kicked off with a clearcut maiden win on the AW (1m) and he then held his own in the Silver Bowl at Carlisle won by Blue Courvoisier; he was only 100-30 for that and unlucky not to be closer than 2l; yet another to bear in mind.
runners.comment: Only 1-7 but strong form when third in the Silver Bowl and he could have been closer

runners.name: Blue Courvoisier
runners.spotlight: Came up shy in his first two handicaps (1m, good) but he was a big market mover when repelling stablemate Langstone in the Silver Bowl at Carlisle (1m, good to firm); that was courtesy of a canny front-running ride by Saffie Osborne and he's gone up 9lb to boot; there's a suspicion that he might have left his Britannia chance in Cumbria.
runners.comment: Dictated up front when winning Carlisle's Silver Bowl and he's gone up 9lb to boot

runners.name: Outback Heat
runners.spotlight: All three runs over 1m under Kaiya Fraser; won on the AW, then third under a penalty; stepped up again when smuggled into his handicap debut from off the pace to win with a bit up his sleeve here in early May (good); it's interesting that Harry Eustace won that same race with his high-class Docklands who came on to win this in 2023; up 6lb but it's likely that further improvement has to be factored in for this well-bred gelding.
runners.comment: C&D winner in May and the trainer's Docklands won that before taking this in 2023

runners.name: Organise
runners.spotlight: Unable to make it 3-3 when a shade of odds-on for his handicap debut at Sandown (1m, good; firmer will be a first) but he gave it a good go behind Laureate Crown and retains significant potential having been gelded in the meantime (now goes in cheekpieces); up 4lb for a defeat but he's been a lively one in the ante-post market; stable won this four times within six years from 1996 but without a win in it since.
runners.comment: 2-3 and gelded since going close on handicap debut; lively in the ante-post market

runners.name: Langstone
runners.spotlight: It's 23 years since Clive Cox won this with New Seeker and this gelding is one of two live hopes for the yard, along with Blue Courvoisier, whom he finished 1.5l behind in the rescheduled Silver Bowl at Carlisle (1m, good to firm); the clear concern is going up 6lb for a defeat and this race is even more competitive.
runners.comment: Steadily progressive but going up 6lb for his defeat in the Silver Bowl is worrying

runners.name: Conclave
runners.spotlight: Awarded his debut race after the first past the post weighed in light; clear second behind a classy rival next time out and then made all in a small field at Hamilton (extended 1m, good); P J McDonald rode him that day; as with so many in here, he's open to improvement on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Sole defeat came at the hands of a classy one; open to improvement, as are many

runners.name: Moonfall
runners.spotlight: Contested a Group 2 last season after winning his novice; gelded in December; his comeback run at Chester was seriously promising when he had to be dropped in from a wide draw, then found trouble up the straight yet was beaten only 2l behind a shorter-priced stablemate; he's way better than that bare form and gets in off the same mark; possibilities.
runners.comment: Gelded in the winter and his comeback run at Chester was seriously promising
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Wechaad
runners.spotlight: Bundles of promise last season even before winning easily on his handicap debut and finishing third in the Group 3 Horris Hill when soft ground might not have been to his liking; his reappearance offered lots of hope for the future, finishing fourth of 17 at Goodwood (7f, good) behind horses with kinder draws and nothing was finishing stronger than this colt; running style and pedigree shouts improvement over this longer trip; he might be the one.
runners.comment: Bundles of promise last season and there's more to come judged on his comeback fourth

runners.name: Victory Tip
runners.spotlight: Disappointed in a Derby trial after winning his maiden (1m, heavy) but soon back in the groove when runner-up at Naas (1m, good) to one who was completing a hat-trick; the winner did him for toe that day but the stronger pace of a Britannia could suit.
runners.comment: Lightly raced gelding who returned to form last time and a truly run Britannia could suit

runners.name: New Monarch
runners.spotlight: Doesn't appear to have made any striking progress as a 3yo and he was beaten fair and square on handicap debut at Goodwood last time in a race contested by others in here who didn't get the rub of the green that day; looks vulnerable.
runners.comment: Doesn't appear to have made any striking improvement as a 3yo; vulnerable

runners.name: Jamestown
runners.spotlight: Close up in a 7f maiden (soft) and 1m handicap (good) before making the most of a good opportunity on the AW (7f maiden; at 8-11); there are some reasons for optimism with his steadily progressive profile.
runners.comment: There are some reasons for optimism with his steadily progressive profile

runners.name: Runman
runners.spotlight: One of three runners for the Gosden yard; Frankel colt; two novice wins on good ground (beat St Anton on debut) and had a double penalty when close up at Nottingham (good to soft); lacks handicap experience but so too did last year's winner Arabian Story; every chance of serving up a personal best and he's tongue tied for the first time.
runners.comment: Sole defeat came under double penalty; last year's winner also had no handicap experience

runners.name: Intense Vision
runners.spotlight: Won on soft (6.5f) and very soft (1m) in France prior to 60,000gns sale; faced promising horses when last of three in an AW conditions race at Southwell in April and shouldn't be knocked for that, but it's probably true that others in this field are more intriguing.
runners.comment: Dual French winner; last of three in an AW conditions race latest (front two are useful)

runners.name: Noelan Star
runners.spotlight: Looked good in winning a pair of 7f/1m novices on Newcastle AW and he was conceding a stone to the front two when third under two penalties at Doncaster (1m, good to firm); inexperienced for a race of this nature but on the flip side he's dangerously unexposed.
runners.comment: Inexperienced for a race of this nature but brings potential into handicaps

runners.name: Flushing Meadows
runners.spotlight: All six runs on good or good to yielding ground; runner-up in small-field Group 3s last season and returned to something like that level when beaten 3l (held up) in a competitive 1m1f Listed race at Navan; however, when that was only 11 days ago this hardly smacks of a masterplan; sports headgear for the first time.
runners.comment: Ran well in a Listed race 11 days ago but this hardly smacks of a masterplan
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Capall Rasa
runners.spotlight: 7f/1m AW wins late last year; given every chance when 3l behind Laureate Crown at Sandown two runs back (1m, good) and then failed to make any impression in Newbury's always-strong London Gold Cup; pulling hard was the last thing he needed when upped to 1m2f in the latter but it wasn't the ideal preparation for this tough a test.
runners.comment: Pulled too hard over 1m2f latest; it wasn't the ideal preparation for this tough test

runners.name: Lion Of Alba
runners.spotlight: Made it third-time lucky last season in a 7.5f novice at Chester (good to firm); however, he was only seventh in the Italian 2,000 Guineas (good) in his comeback race and that casts a shadow over his prospects.
runners.comment: Well held in the Italian 2,000 Guineas and that casts a shadow over his prospects

runners.name: Vincenzo Peruggia
runners.spotlight: Last season's novice win came on good to firm (7.5f); this year's defeats at Musselburgh (1m, good to soft; ran well) and Newmarket (good to firm; not so well from the front) mark him down as an unlikely winner of this.
runners.comment: Below par from the front at Newmarket last time; makes limited appeal on the back of that

runners.name: Pathein
runners.spotlight: He did have an entry in the St James's Palace and retains one in the Irish Derby; close up in a Group 3 last season (1m, good to firm) and ran well under top weight to be third of 17 on handicap debut at Goodwood (7f, good) where he was nearest at the finish after hitting traffic; by no means exposed and he is very much respected.
runners.comment: Unlucky third on handicap debut; by no means exposed and he is very much respected

runners.name: Richie's Rocket
runners.spotlight: All runs 7f, the win in an AW maiden in November; gelded after disappointing in a classy conditions race and his handicap debut at Goodwood (good) last time was fairly eye-catching when he had a wicked draw and saw his race out well (behind Pathein, Wechaad and New Monarch); not dismissed.
runners.comment: Gelded prior to handicap debut at Goodwood where he was drawn wide and finished well

runners.name: Laureate Crown
runners.spotlight: Won on good to firm here on debut but bypassed the Silver Bowl at Carlisle last month on account of those conditions; he'd probably run well if okay on this surface, having gone 2-2 since being gelded and last time at Sandown (1m, good) looking good value for his narrow defeat of Organise.
runners.comment: 2-2 since gelded and he looked good value for his narrow defeat of Organise at Sandown

runners.name: Winding Stream
runners.spotlight: Made a highly encouraging start on the AW before suffering a reality check in Sandown's Group 3 Classic Trial (behind Wise Prince); it could be that he never did himself true justice on that occasion but he did go off at 80-1.
runners.comment: Good start on the AW but well beaten in a Group 3 latest when 80-1

runners.name: Wise Prince
runners.spotlight: Promising 2yo winner; yet to get in the thick of things this season but started off in Group races and he ultimately paid for chasing a strong pace when folding late on in a good Listed event at Sandown three weeks ago (1m, good; unraced on faster); no forlorn hope on handicap debut with Toby Moore taking 7lb off this back.
runners.comment: This is a welcome drop in grade, albeit in a red-hot handicap; not yet exposed

runners.name: New Connection
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced French raider who won his second race over 1m1f on soft ground and posted a respectable RPR (with a view to this initial mark) when beaten 3l last time over 1m3f (good to soft); has to be of some interest for his powerful connections, for all that this trip and ground will be a first for him; tries a tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Bit to prove as regards trip and ground, but represents powerful connections

runners.name: Exclusive Code
runners.spotlight: Encouraging runs behind Group-class horses last season; back from a lengthy break and a gelding operation when the workmanlike winner of a big-field maiden at Newbury (1m, good) that's produced a stack of winners; unexposed and respected.
runners.comment: Won big-field Newbury maiden that's produced a stack of winners; unexposed and respected

runners.name: Hilitany
runners.spotlight: Won two 6f novice races last season (good to firm/good) and added 7f Listed honours on the AW in February, after which he was gelded; never really sighted when behind some of these on handicap debut at Goodwood (7f; 18-1) but that was after nearly three months away; entitled to run better this afternoon but others are probably on kinder marks.
runners.comment: Three-time winner; behind some of these when never involved on handicap debut latest

races.race_name: Hampton Court Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Morshdi
runners.spotlight: Won at York (1m, good) last October on second start and reappeared with Listed win at Newmarket (1m1f, good) in April; only fifth in the Group 2 Dante at York (10.2f, good) last month and he needs to raise his game, but he may be capable of better than he showed on that latest start; not discounted.
runners.comment: Listed win and Group 2 Dante fifth; needs to raise his game but may be capable of better

runners.name: Endorsement
runners.spotlight: Cheekpieces fitted in last two races; second to subsequent Derby winner Christmas Day in Group 3 Ballysax at Leopardstown (1m2f, good to yielding) in April and again finished close up in Group 3 Derby Trial over same C&D (good) before easy Listed win from the front there (1m4f, soft) a fortnight ago; holds leading form claims.
runners.comment: Went close in two Group 3s at Leopardstown before easy Listed win there; leading claims

runners.name: Maho Bay
runners.spotlight: Won on debut at Kempton (1m3f, AW) last December and followed up in Newmarket novice (1m2f, good) in April, again with something to spare; unplaced favourite in the Lingfield Derby Trial (11.6f, good to firm) but his top yard was going through a quiet spell and he's not written off.
runners.comment: 2-2 before unplaced in Lingfield Derby Trial, but top yard was going through a quiet spell

runners.name: Generic
runners.spotlight: Went close on debut in March (1m, AW) and won by 3l at Yarmouth (1m, good) in April; beaten 7l when second of five in Listed race at Chester (10.3f, good) last month but it's not just the winner Constitution River who has franked that form, and he still looked a work in progress; interesting.
runners.comment: Beaten 7l when Listed 2nd at Chester but that run still reads well; interesting contender

runners.name: Oxagon
runners.spotlight: Won the Group 3 Craven at Newmarket (1m, good) in April in first-time cheekpieces (retained) then kept on for respectable sixth in the 2,000 Guineas there (1m, good to firm); faded when 12th of 16 in the Group 1 Prix du Jockey Club at Chantilly (10.5f, good) but this trip may well be within range if he settles better; not ruled out back down in grade under 4lb penalty.
runners.comment: Craven winner; faded over 10.5f latest but trip may be within range if he settles better

runners.name: My Love Is King
runners.spotlight: Won last November on sole 2yo start (7f, AW) and runner-up on both this term (1m2f, good/good to firm), most recently a Listed race at Newmarket; unexposed and his pedigree provides optimism that he will continue to progress for his top stable; not ruled out in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Listed runner-up last time; pedigree provides optimism that he will continue to progress

runners.name: Italy
runners.spotlight: 0-5 since winning on debut at Leopardstown (7f, good) last May and he could do with settling better, while Ryan Moore rides stablemate Endorsement; however, he's run some big races in defeat and could build on April's reappearance fourth in the Group 3 Ballysax at Leopardstown (1m2f, good to yielding; Endorsement second); might have a part to play.
runners.comment: Headstrong; could build on reappearance 4th in the Ballysax and might have a part to play

runners.name: Mountain Cat
runners.spotlight: Made debut last month and he's 2-2, with comfortable novice wins at Doncaster (1m, good to firm) and Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW); takes a steep rise in grade but subsequent winners have emerged from both of his races and this Teofilo colt could be suited by the step up in trip; on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Made debut last month and he's 2-2; steep rise in grade but could be up to the task

runners.name: Glacius
runners.spotlight: Won on debut at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) last August before Listed fourth at Haydock (1m, good), and he rallied for close third in the Group 3 Autumn Stakes at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) last October when last seen; he's the only one of these who hasn't run this year and it's a tough assignment on his comeback, but his latest form reads well and he has potential at 1m2f.
runners.comment: Close third in Group 3 last October; absent since but that form reads well; has potential

runners.name: Oceans Four
runners.spotlight: Went very close in the Group 3 Solario at Sandown (7f, soft) last August and won the Group 3 Prix des Chenes at Chantilly (1m, soft) in September; his trainer won this two years ago and this 3yo is much better than he showed in the Group 3 Classic Trial at Sandown (1m2f, good) in April on his reappearance, but he's hard to fancy on the back of that heavy defeat (50-1).
runners.comment: French Group 3 winner last September but heavy defeat at 50-1 in Sandown Classic Trial

races.race_name: Buckingham Palace Stakes (Handicap)

runners.name: Dance In The Storm
runners.spotlight: Developing into a useful 7f filly and did well to win at Chester (good) on seasonal debut, having been drawn wide and come from quite a difficult position; below-par favourite at Epsom on softer ground since but it may be best to forgive that effort; still of interest.
runners.comment: Developing into a useful 7f filly; may be best to forgive latest effort; still of interest

runners.name: The Lost King
runners.spotlight: Gelded prior to showing improvement and performing solidly (12134) over 7f-1m1f this year, mostly on AW, including a good effort on finals day at Newcastle; respectable fourth at Newmarket returned to turf and again looks likely to give his running.
runners.comment: Gelded prior to performing solidly (12134) this year; should give another good account

runners.name: River King
runners.spotlight: Gained his 2025 novice win over 1m2f but coped well with the return to 1m at Newbury (good to firm) most recently, comfortably making all and giving the impression he'll be as effective dropped back even further in trip; low-mileage 4yo who is improving and is by a sire whose biggest moment came on the straight track at Ascot, for this stable; major claims.
runners.comment: Comfortably made all at Newbury last time; low-mileage 4yo who is improving; good claims

runners.name: Mezcala
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who has raced mostly over 1m, showing broadly progressive form; comfortably landed the Lincoln consolation race at Doncaster (good to soft) on seasonal debut, then ran creditably at Newbury; gives the impression he'll be fine over this stiff 7f; may do better still.
runners.comment: Broadly progressive miler who gives the impression he'll be fine over this stiff 7f

runners.name: Rock Of Cashel
runners.spotlight: Proved well down the pecking order at Ballydoyle, notwithstanding two wins (7f/1m2f); comfortably on top in Ovrevoll handicap (8.5f) in latest start for a Norwegian stable that he has joined this year; however, faces a markedly harder assignment in this field.
runners.comment: Has joined a Norwegian stable this year; comfortably on top last time but this is harder

runners.name: So Darn Hot
runners.spotlight: Four-time 6f winner who ran respectably in first-time cheekpieces (retained today) in the Victoria Cup over C&D most recently, albeit taking record for current yard to 0-5 and failing to improve for a return to 7f.
runners.comment: Ran respectably in the Victoria Cup, without improving for a return to 7f; all wins at 6f

runners.name: Cosi Bello
runners.spotlight: Found a gap on the inside and knuckled down well to justify favouritism at Haydock (good to firm) on sole start this term, taking overall record (AW/turf) to 3-5 and form figures over 7f to 1121; looks the type to improve further and commands respect up just 2lb.
runners.comment: Reappearance win at Haydock took his record to 3-5; the type to improve further; respected

runners.name: Colombier
runners.spotlight: Possibly best on ground softer than good; pulled too hard over 1m at Ascot on stable/seasonal debut; settled better dropped back to 7f at Epsom on Oaks day and prevailed by neck, returning to form and taking record over this trip to 3-5; further success is plausible.
runners.comment: Prevailed at Epsom on Oaks day, taking 7f record to 3-5; further success is plausible

runners.name: Royal Velvet
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mainly on good/firmer; progressive and brings impressive stats, being 9-21 overall, 2-2 this season, 6-10 over 7f and 4-5 under William Buick; well on top in big-field handicap at Newmarket on reappearance and followed up in Lingfield Group 3; lots to like about her.
runners.comment: Progressive mare; 9-21 overall, 2-2 this term, 6-10 over 7f and 4-5 under William Buick

runners.name: Apiarist
runners.spotlight: Eye-catcher, amid traffic issues, in the Thirsk Hunt Cup last time; form over C&D comprises a respectable eighth (off current mark) in this contest last year and creditable third (did well having been hampered) later in 2025; still has a much better strike-rate on AW than on turf, however.
runners.comment: Respectable eighth in this contest last year but still has a modest strike-rate on turf

runners.name: Defence Minister
runners.spotlight: Record is 3-10; gelded prior to reappearance success at Kempton (7f, AW; looked better than ever) and had an excuse (raced in unfavoured group) when never landing a blow in the Victoria Cup over C&D since; could go well, provided this race pans out better.
runners.comment: Gelded prior to reappearance success; raced in unfavoured group in Victoria Cup since

runners.name: Hickory
runners.spotlight: Has plenty of good form at Ascot and his most recent course effort (when bidding for a second win in the Victoria Cup) can be excused as the race didn't pan out favourably for him; however, he has a difficult-looking mark to overcome in this first Royal Ascot attempt.
runners.comment: Has plenty of Ascot form but runs off a difficult mark in this first Royal Ascot attempt

runners.name: Storm Star
runners.spotlight: Has gained all wins on Newcastle AW, the most recent on finals day last year; however, ran well at Ascot (1m, good to firm) returned to turf last time, taking form figures in this sphere to 3232; seems effective over 7f and may remain competitive.
runners.comment: Ran well at Ascot returned to turf last time, taking form figures in this sphere to 3232

runners.name: Mirsky
runners.spotlight: Only midfield off current mark in this race 12 months ago, despite arriving at the top of his game; has been in worse form this time, well below par in both starts since landing the Thirsk Hunt Cup; has done all his winning over further than 7f and can be opposed.
runners.comment: Only midfield off current mark in this race 12 months ago; yet to win over 7f

runners.name: Pellitory
runners.spotlight: Proved rather difficult to place last term after a reappearance win that did major damage to his mark; perhaps needed the run when 40-1 in the Victoria Cup over C&D on stable/seasonal debut but is best watched unless the market signals suggest he'll take a step forward.
runners.comment: Perhaps needed the run when 40-1 in the Victoria Cup over C&D on stable/seasonal debut

runners.name: Stratusnine
runners.spotlight: Finished strongly from a difficult position in 5f contest at York last time and nearly got up, looking in need of a return to further; however, he may find this stiff 7f too much of a stretch and still needs to prove he can win over distances other than 6f.
runners.comment: Finished strongly over 5f last time but may find this stiff 7f too much of a stretch

runners.name: Nizam
runners.spotlight: Possibly needs ground softer than good; successful three times (7.5f/1m) in France but has been out of the money in two British runs, albeit not disgraced at Epsom (8.5f) latest; doesn't look as if he's about to land a handicap of this nature.
runners.comment: Ex-French 4yo who has been out of the money in two British runs; others preferred

runners.name: Fondo Blanco
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive and has a largely solid record; won well in his last handicap attempt, then ran well in Listed grade; gelded since; has C&D form and looks an interesting contender on seasonal debut, with further improvement a possibility; market support should be heeded.
runners.comment: Steadily progressive and has a largely solid record; interesting contender on reappearance

runners.name: Arctic Dawn
runners.spotlight: Arrives in top form having won really well under Zac Lloyd at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) last time, building on his reappearance effort and taking record in cheekpieces to 4-10; that sets him up perfectly for a crack at a bigger prize and further progress is not out of the question; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Arrives in top form, having won really well under Zac Lloyd at Doncaster last time

runners.name: Brasil Power
runners.spotlight: Effective at 7f earlier in career but seems to need much further now and gained last three wins over 1m4f/1m6f (stays 2m); still has a much better strike-rate on AW than on turf in any case and looks unlikely to figure in this hot prize, back down sharply in distance.
runners.comment: Gained last three wins over 1m4f/1m6f; needs to prove he still has the toe for 7f

runners.name: Great Acclaim
runners.spotlight: Versatile as regards ground; has a good record in blinkers and ran well switched to a visor (retained today) last time, finishing only 1l behind The Wizard Of Eye in the Victoria Cup and taking form figures over C&D to 242; solid contender who again has frame possibilities.
runners.comment: Has a broadly good record in headgear; second in the Victoria Cup over C&D most recently

runners.name: Golden Mind
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a rival in Group race last time; seemed to run well in the Victoria Cup over C&D in his most recent handicap attempt but was always favourably positioned the way the race went and needs to prove he can back up the form; still on career-high mark.
runners.comment: Seemed to run well in the Victoria Cup but was always favourably positioned

runners.name: The Fingal Raven
runners.spotlight: Quite a useful sort but has a stronger record at Meydan than on home soil and his limitations were rather exposed, even allowing for a tricky draw, in the Britannia at this meeting 12 months ago; wears first-time tongue-tie; something to prove back in a British race.
runners.comment: Has a stronger record at Meydan than on home soil; something to prove

runners.name: Blue Brother
runners.spotlight: Trained in France in 2024 then Qatar last year; absent and gelded since meeting traffic issues when 18th of 30 in the 2025 Royal Hunt Cup (attracted support);  drops to a bare 7f for first time but the stiff track and likely strong pace will help; market confidence should be heeded.
runners.comment: Met traffic issues in the 2025 Royal Hunt Cup; first run since; should cope with stiff 7f

runners.name: Room Service
runners.spotlight: Group placed over 7f last June but rather lost his form afterwards in 2025; sold out of Kevin Ryan's yard for 72,000gns and gelded since last run; wears first-time tongue-tie and blinkers; presented with a tough task off top weight in a hot handicap on stable/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Presented with a tough task off top weight in a hot handicap on stable/seasonal debut

runners.name: The Wizard Of Eye
runners.spotlight: An ideal type for this race having won the Victoria Cup over C&D in 2024 and 2026, both times off 5lb lower on good ground (lightly raced in between and latest occasion was only his third run for new yard); still favourably treated on historical data; good credentials.
runners.comment: An ideal type for this race, having won the Victoria Cup over C&D in 2024 and 2026
runners.owner: R Humphrey O Humphrey A Favell

runners.name: Elarak
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who was 3-5 last term, readily winning at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) final start; hindered by racing alone returned to that C&D last time; may still have more to offer and can't be written off; Billy Loughnane (up for first time) is a booking of note.
runners.comment: Best to forgive latest effort; may still have more to offer and can't be written off

runners.name: English Oak
runners.spotlight: Campaigned at Meydan this year for new yard, winning two handicaps then not disgraced in Group 3; two attempts at this prize comprise a ready success off 4lb lower in 2024 (good to firm; recorded peak RPR) and poor effort (in cheekpieces) last year; likely player provided he's in top form.
runners.comment: Two attempts at this prize include a ready win off 4lb lower in 2024 (recorded peak RPR)

runners.name: Vafortino
runners.spotlight: Landed the Victoria Cup over C&D in 2022 and hasn't had the smoothest of passages in two attempts in this contest (finished 11th that year and tenth in 2023); losing run is mounting up, however, and he doesn't look particularly solid on his form for current stable.
runners.comment: Not particularly solid on his form for current stable; third attempt at this race
runners.owner: D MacAuliffe, Anoj Don & Dahab Racing

races.race_name: Ripon Racecourse Ladies' Derby Handicap (For Female Amateur Jockeys) (Special Rider Conditions)

runners.name: Kitsune Power
runners.spotlight: No win since June 2024 (Thirsk, 1m6f; from 15lb higher); consistent this year, going closest when runner-up at Beverley (1m2f, good to firm) three weeks ago; trainer has won this three times in the last four runnings.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Beverley (1m2f, good to firm) latest; trainer has solid record in this race.

runners.name: Jack Langley
runners.spotlight: Three Lingfield AW wins (1m4f-1m5f) for Simon Dow; changed hands in April for 11,000gns and only time will tell how he fares for his new handler; relatively unexposed on turf (0-3) but may strip fitter for this off an 83-day break.
runners.comment: Three AW wins for previous yard; 0-3 on turf and may strip fitter for this after a break

runners.name: Apache Eagle
runners.spotlight: Placed on numerous occasions this year, including when runner-up at Southwell (1m6f, AW) in April, but continues to seek that elusive first win (0-19); reverts to turf, with Serena Brotherton a positive jockey booking.
runners.comment: Often placed but continues to seek elusive first win (0-19); Serena Brotherton is booked.

runners.name: One Million Dreams
runners.spotlight: Won twice for Nick Littmoden, including over 1m4f at Brighton (good) last August; low-key form since joining this trainer in May for 6,000gns and has plenty to prove at present.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way in two runs for this yard and has plenty to prove at present.

runners.name: Bruce Banner
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden hurdle at Musselburgh last November; did his best work late when runner-up at Redcar (1m6f, good) on penultimate start; slightly below that level when third (beaten 5l) over this trip at Beverley subsequently.
runners.comment: Runner-up over 1m6f at Redcar penultimate start; slightly below that level latest.

runners.name: Bouboule
runners.spotlight: 19-race maiden; runner-up at Newcastle (12.5f, AW) in January, but no identifiable positives since; swiftly reverts to the Flat after well held over hurdles at Perth 11 days ago.
runners.comment: 19-race maiden; runner-up at Newcastle (12.5f) in January, no identifiable positives since

runners.name: Yakhabar
runners.spotlight: Sole turf win in this race last year; in reasonable form on AW at Southwell in the spring but his first run since wind surgery, on Newcastle AW, was a poor effort.
runners.comment: Sole turf win in this race last year; in fair form on AW in the spring; modest latest

runners.name: Max Of Stars
runners.spotlight: 117-rated hurdler, who regularly took travelling in her stride (three wins down at Newton Abbot); won well, making all, on the Flat at Thirsk (1m4f, soft; from 7lb lower) last September; notable candidate back to this discipline following more wind surgery.
runners.comment: 117-rated hurdler; won well, making all, at Thirsk (1m4f) last September; one to consider.
runners.owner: Mrs S D Pearson & Ollie Pears

runners.name: Arranmore
runners.spotlight: No turf win since August 2021; kept on well when winning on AW at Southwell (1m3f; from 3lb lower) in March; perhaps found 1m (here) and 1m2f at Pontefract on the sharp side since; Becky Smith now takes the mount.
runners.comment: Won on AW at Southwell (1m3f; 3lb lower) in March; well held over shorter the last twice.

runners.name: Pride Of Nepal
runners.spotlight: Completed the hat-trick at Chepstow (1m2f, good to soft) on Monday; carries a 4lb penalty for that and  stays 1m4f fine, so likely to have a major role to play again under Sarah Bowen, if electing to stand his ground.
runners.comment: Completed the hat-trick at Chepstow (1m2f, good to soft) on Monday; carries a 4lb penalty.

runners.name: Speechman
runners.spotlight: Yet to win for this trainer (0-16), in rear throughout on last visit to this track (1m2f) in May; subsequent Newcastle effort no better; stablemate of Yurinov; far better to focus elsewhere.
runners.comment: Yet to win for this trainer (0-16); far better to focus elsewhere.

runners.name: Yurinov
runners.spotlight: 27-race maiden; has raced exclusively on AW since finishing remote last of nine to Max Of Stars at Thirsk (1m4f) last September; modest fourth (beaten 11l) to Yakhabar when last seen at Southwell in March; unappealing stablemate of Speechman.
runners.comment: 0-27; only fourth (btn 11l) to Yakhabar when last seen at Southwell in March; unappealing.

races.race_name: Live For Today Best Dressed Gentleman EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (B/C/D) (Qual') (GBB/IRE Inc')

runners.name: Super Alpha
runners.spotlight: Third to the still unbeaten Adonius on Musselburgh debut (5f, soft; 8-1, beaten over 7l) in early April; bettered that on RPRs (70) when weakening fifth back there three weeks later; returns from 53-day absence as a gelding; needs only slightly more to make it third-time lucky.
runners.comment: Back from 53-day break as a gelding; needs only slightly more to make it third-time lucky.

runners.name: State Of Gold
runners.spotlight: £20,000 yearling; fared a bit better at York (6f; 80-1) last week on third start, when seventh of 15 to Everatease in the competitive Rous Selling Stakes; will need to kick on again, though, to make her mark in this novice contest.
runners.comment: Fared a bit better at York (6f seller; 80-1; seventh of 15) last week on third start.

runners.name: Inns And Out
runners.spotlight: 6,000euros yearling; third foal from a modest dam; late headway after a slow start, when fifth of seven on Pontefract debut (6f maiden; 40-1, beaten nearly 4l) over a fortnight ago; progression is possible, and far more likely than stablemate Carry on Chaos.
runners.comment: Late headway when fifth on Pontefract debut (6f maiden; 40-1, btn 4l) over a fortnight ago

runners.name: Wong Fei Hong
runners.spotlight: 29,000euros yearling; tenth foal from a once-raced dam; half-brother to four French winners, including Spanish Fly (Group-placed 6f 2yo; RPR 98); trainer saddled a winning newcomer (Princesse d'Orange) at Beverley in April, so this youngster is worthy of close market scrutiny.
runners.comment: 29,000euros yearling; tenth foal from once-raced dam; half-brother to four French winners.

runners.name: Johns Power
runners.spotlight: 8,000gns yearling; first foal from an unraced dam who is a half-sister to two winners, most notably Frizzy Friday (6.5f-8.5f including 2yo); his doubly represented trainer is 12-185 (6%) with 2yos over the past five years.
runners.comment: 8,000gns yearling; first foal from an unraced dam who is a half-sister to two winners.

runners.name: Persazz
runners.spotlight: 55,000gns yearling; sixth foal from Listed-placed 5f/5.7f winner (RPR 96); half-brother to two winners, Feeraas (7f 2yo; 77), and Belon (French 9.5f 2yo); trainer is 19-187 (10%) with juveniles over the last five years.
runners.comment: 55,000gns yearling; sixth foal from Listed-placed 5f/5.7f winner (RPR 96); market helpful.

runners.name: Boutblummintime
runners.spotlight: Respectable sixth of 15 in the Brocklesby (18-1, beaten 8l), but took a sharp backward step at Hamilton (6f) next time; gelded since; best watched, with a view to nurseries forthcoming.
runners.comment: Sharp backward step at Hamilton; gelded since; best watched, with a view to nurseries.

runners.name: Bigalo
runners.spotlight: 16,000gns yearling; slowly away, always behind, on Redcar debut (5f maiden, good to firm; 11-1) 24 days ago; gelded since, hoping for better on second appearance; stablemate of newcomer Johns Power.
runners.comment: 16,000gns yearling; slowly away, always behind, on Redcar debut (5f maiden); gelded since.

runners.name: Pj's Corner
runners.spotlight: £30,000 yearling; last of eight (beaten 27l) on debut at Lingfield (7f maiden, good to firm; 14-1) 19 days ago; this stablemate of Printing Money needs to leave that well behind to stake a claim.
runners.comment: Last of eight (btn 27l) on debut at Lingfield (7f maiden, good to firm; 14-1) 19 days ago.

runners.name: Carry On Chaos
runners.spotlight: Has failed to threaten to get competitive in two starts, at Redcar and Nottingham (40-1 and 150-1); no reason to expect better from this stablemate of Inns And Out.
runners.comment: Has failed to threaten to get competitive in two starts, at Redcar and Nottingham.

runners.name: Printing Money
runners.spotlight: 50,000gns yearling; ran green, off the bridle from an early stage, failing to justify market support on Windsor debut (6f, good to firm; 3-1 favourite, ninth of 12, beaten over 8l) under a month ago; Kevin Stott takes over in the saddle; stablemate of Pj's Corner.
runners.comment: Failed to justify market support on Windsor debut (6f; 3-1 favourite, ninth, btn over 8l).
runners.owner: Hold My Beer, N Bradley 6, L Bearman

races.race_name: Grantley Hall Handicap

runners.name: Qitaal
runners.spotlight: 3-35; drops into a Class 5 for the first time since his juvenile days; didn't have a fair crack at it when runner-up at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm; 9-4 favourite) on penultimate start, but no excuses at Hamilton latest (fifth), failing to fully see it out; could bounce back.
runners.comment: 3-35; eased in grade; no excuses at Hamilton latest (fifth), failing to fully see it out.

runners.name: Yermanthere
runners.spotlight: No win since August 2024, and he's yet to score beyond 7.5f, with a Flat record of 0-5 at 8.5f plus; this easing mark is helpful though, and he wasn't too far behind the reopposing King Of Fury at Carlisle (1m1f, soft; 6-1) ten days ago; stablemate of the veteran Poets Dawn.
runners.comment: Easing mark is helpful; wasn't too far behind King Of Fury at Carlisle (1m1f) latest.

runners.name: What's The Plan
runners.spotlight: Won in newly applied headgear at Lingfield (7f AW maiden; 7-4) last September; two respectable efforts this year since a gelding operation, close third to Mayaada at Salisbury (1m2f, good) latest; Arron D'Arcy retains the mount; chance.
runners.comment: Close third to Mayaada at Salisbury (1m2f, good) latest; Arron D'Arcy retains the mount.

runners.name: Saxophonist
runners.spotlight: Third to two better-fancied rivals on seasonal reappearance at Doncaster (1m, good to firm) in April, and occupied that same position in a three-runner affair at Hamilton (8.5f) subsequently; a glance at his pedigree suggests that tackling this longer trip isn't an obvious move.
runners.comment: Last of three at Hamilton (8.5f; 11-8 favourite) latest; stamina a risk factor over 1m2f.

runners.name: King Of Fury
runners.spotlight: Three AW wins, the latest by a neck at Kempton (1m; from 4lb higher) this time last year; yet to win on turf (0-13), but did finish one place ahead of Yermanthere at Carlisle (1m1f, soft; 5-1) ten days ago; that form was boosted when third-placed Star Cast won easily at Chester on Saturday.
runners.comment: Three AW wins, the latest at Kempton (1m; from 4lb higher) this time last year; 0-13 turf.
runners.owner: Mrs K S Pratt & M Dods

runners.name: Roc De Fer
runners.spotlight: Modest in three AW runs in novice company, a weakening fourth of nine at Chelmsford (1m; 28-1) last December; gelded and tackles a new trip, off a six-month absence, on this turf and handicap debut; his dam was Group-placed over 1m1f and a 2m hurdle winner, so improvement could be forthcoming.
runners.comment: 0-3; gelded and tackles a new trip, off a six-month absence, on this turf/handicap debut.

runners.name: Poet's Dawn
runners.spotlight: Best when allowed to dominate, so this track suits his style of running; two wins already this year, here and at Musselburgh (1m1f); sits 3lb above his last winning mark, but still posted a creditable effort in a stronger race than this at York (sixth of 18; 28-1) last week.
runners.comment: Two wins already this year, here and at Musselburgh (1m1f); 3lb above last winning mark.

races.race_name: Ripon Races Ladies' Day Handicap

runners.name: Wild Clary
runners.spotlight: Has been in good form in 6f handicaps this season, including second over C&D in April (good to soft) and third at Carlisle in May (good to firm); won his first race on turf when prevailing at Newbury last week (good to soft); has a penalty but sound chance.
runners.comment: In good form this term; has a penalty for last week's Newbury win; goes well here

runners.name: Vince L'Amour
runners.spotlight: Won a C&D handicap in April 2024 (heavy); has run mainly over 5f since; fair run in a decent 5f handicap at Carlisle last week (soft); each-way chance.
runners.comment: C&D winner in 2024; all other wins at 5f; fair run last week at Carlisle; each-way chance

runners.name: Miraculous
runners.spotlight: Chester winner (6f, good) for Roger Varian last season; best runs for Tim Easterby have been over same C&D (third there on Saturday after racing wide); chance on that last run but not sure this straight 6f will be ideal.
runners.comment: Chester winner for Roger Varian last term and two good runs there this term; could go well

runners.name: Dark Thirty
runners.spotlight: Ex-Richard Hannon; off the mark for the Quinn team in a 22-runner handicap off 6lb lower at York in May (6f, good); lesser run at Thirsk last time; could bounce back
runners.comment: Ex-Richard Hannon; won 22-runner York h'cap in May; not so good latest; could bounce back

runners.name: Dark Cloud Rising
runners.spotlight: Won three times last year (5f/6f, good/good to firm/AW), the third when wearing cheekpieces for the first time; disappointing this season, last time unseating his rider at the start in first-time visor which is retained.
runners.comment: Three wins last season, the third in cheekpieces; disappointing this term; visored

runners.name: Strong Warrior
runners.spotlight: Shaped well on his first four career starts, winning an Ayr maiden (5f, good) and a Pontefract novice (6f, good); disappointing since, including on first run after being gelded (AW) last time; back after a break.
runners.comment: Won two of his first four races, but has been disappointing since; plenty to prove

runners.name: Another Baar
runners.spotlight: Three course wins, two over 6f, the other at 5f; this season's win was a 13-runner event at Bahrain in January (5f, good to firm); hasn't run quite as well since, but may have needed it when 5.25l tenth of 20 in the Epsom Dash last time (5f, good to soft; 66-1); a possible.
runners.comment: Winner in Bahrain in January; has not run as well since; should be better for latest run

races.race_name: Masons Gin 'Who Dares, Gins' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Eklleem
runners.spotlight: Best run when second in a Newmarket novice last October (7f, good; favourite; third and fourth have won since); sent off at 10-11 when close fourth in a Newcastle novice early last month; 1m should suit and has a chance.
runners.comment: Sound efforts when second at Newmarket and fourth at Newcastle; 1m should suit; chance

runners.name: Ervani
runners.spotlight: Stepped up on his debut second at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) when fourth in a York novice last month (1m, good; third has won since); should go well again.
runners.comment: Two good runs, second at Wetherby and fourth at York, both at 1m; should go well again

runners.name: Ceinture d'Orion
runners.spotlight: Fair fourth in a Wetherby fillies' novice on debut (1m2f, good to firm) but tailed off on soft ground at Haydock next time; place chance.
runners.comment: Fourth on Wetherby debut (good to firm); tailed off on soft ground last time; place chance

runners.name: Jetpack Jimmy
runners.spotlight: Home-bred by Masar; half-brother to 1m2f winner out of a very useful 1m2f/1m4f winner; likely to improve for this debut run.
runners.comment: Home-bred half-brother to a 1m2f winner out of a 1m2f/1m4f winner; likely to need the run

runners.name: Carnival Of Light
runners.spotlight: Godolphin-bred; bought back unraced for 1,000gns in October; by Night Of Thunder; first foal of a 9.4f AW/1m2f turf winner; quite interesting on belated debut.
runners.comment: Bred by Godolphin; bought back for 1,000gns in October; quite interesting on belated debut

runners.name: Ziggy Bay
runners.spotlight: Well beaten over 6f and 7f on her two runs and looks one for handicaps.
runners.comment: Well beaten over 6f and 7f on her two runs and looks one for handicaps

runners.name: Stolen Kisses
runners.spotlight: Dark Angel filly; 50-1 when a well-beaten fifth in fillies' maiden on debut at Carlisle last week (1m, soft); best watched for now.
runners.comment: Dark Angel filly; well beaten 50-1 shot on last week's Carlisle debut; best watched

races.race_name: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap

runners.name: Liosa
runners.spotlight: Long-distance raider; AW winner but 0-11 on turf; far from disgraced when 2.25l sixth of 14 at Windsor last time (6f, good to firm); each-way chance.
runners.comment: AW winner who is 0-11 on turf; decent sixth of 14 at Windsor last time; each-way chance

runners.name: Federal Envoy
runners.spotlight: All three wins have been over 7f at Southwell for Karl Burke then Iain Jardine; has dropped to a fair mark after some disappointing runs recently and now makes debut for new yard after changing hands for 7,000gns in April; not sure 6f on turf is ideal.
runners.comment: All wins over 7f at Southwell; not sure 6f on turf is ideal; first run for new stable

runners.name: Mr Cool
runners.spotlight: Winner off a 12lb higher mark at this meeting last year (6f, good); mainly disappointing since, two best runs being on standard to slow surface at Kempton (including over 7f); well treated; others look stronger.
runners.comment: C&D win off 12lb lower in June last year; mainly disappointing since; others look stronger

runners.name: Fircombe Hall
runners.spotlight: Only one of his ten wins has been on turf (record of 1-27); beaten less than 2l when fifth of 12 at Redcar on most recent turf run, but poor effort back on AW next time; others preferred.
runners.comment: Record of 1-27 on turf, but not disgraced at Redcar latest turf run; others preferred

runners.name: Big Apple Jack
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden; fair second at Nottingham on reappearance in April (6f, good) but well beaten on AW debut latest; could bounce back.
runners.comment: 0-11; fair 2nd at Nottingham in April and could bounce back from lesser AW run last time

runners.name: Wish This
runners.spotlight: Newcastle winner last November (6f) when blinkered for the first time; hasn't progressed this season, but the form of his latest fifth at Hamilton (6f, good) is working out quite well; has a chance in an ordinary race.
runners.comment: AW winner in November when blinkered; not progressed but form of last run sound; chance

runners.name: Rajstar
runners.spotlight: Unexposed; won a Newcastle handicap in February on only fourth start; has been absent since a disappointing run there ten days later; fair run only previous outing on turf (on debut in Ireland); interesting.
runners.comment: Unexposed; AW win in February; absent since lesser run next time; interesting back on turf

runners.name: Fan Mail
runners.spotlight: Dual 6f winner on the AW; decent efforts last two turf runs, notably when neck second on good to soft at Carlisle last month (6f; cheekpieces on for first time); fair fifth in first-time blinkers at Thirsk last time (6f, good); cheekpieces the preferred option this time; could go well.
runners.comment: Dual 6f AW winner; fair runs on turf with headgear on last two runs; could go well
runners.owner: L Bond/Sheikh Aymen Jawad Al Hamandi/Ptr

races.race_name: Your Harrogate Ladies' Day Handicap

runners.name: Hatamoto
runners.spotlight: Maiden; placed in handicaps at Carlisle (1m, soft; winner and third have won since) and Leicester (7f, good to firm) in May; not so good last time (1m1f, soft); chance.
runners.comment: Maiden; placed in handicaps at Carlisle and Leicester last month; poor run latest; chance

runners.name: Hood Wink
runners.spotlight: Dead-heated in a 1m handicap at Wetherby in April (good to firm); ran another sound race when third to a stable companion over this C&D last time; worth considering.
runners.comment: Dead-heated in a 1m h'cap in April and a good second over C&D last time; worth considering

runners.name: Trucial Pearl
