runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden; has shown fair form on occasions, notably when third at Catterick last time (7f, good); shapes as though this step up to 1m will suit.
runners.comment: 0-11; several fair runs including third at Catterick last time (7f); 1m may suit

runners.name: Regal Knight
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden; relatively unexposed but has a bit to find with Hood Wink on last time's C&D form; cheekpieces now tried.
runners.comment: 0-6; unexposed, but a bit to find with Hood Wink on C&D form last time; headgear now tried
runners.owner: D Bishop P Robinson S Bland T Coyle

runners.name: Raven's View
runners.spotlight: Unexposed; well beaten on all three starts in handicaps, over 7f, 1m2f and last time over 1m4f on turf debut; drops back in trip with cheekpieces tried; bit to prove.
runners.comment: Well beaten on three starts in handicaps from 7f-1m4f; now tried in cheekpieces

runners.name: Shes Got The Blues
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on eighth start on turf in a 7f classified stakes at Leicester ten days ago; stays 1m; has a stiffer task with her penalty in this better race.
runners.comment: Off the mark in classified event at Leicester last time; stiffer task in a better race

runners.name: A Boy Named Mary
runners.spotlight: Modest form so far, including in three handicaps, although spoilt his chance when ducking sharply away at the start when a fair eighth to a stable companion at Redcar last time (7f, good to firm); bit to prove
runners.comment: Modest form in three h'caps, although lost his chance at the start last time; bit to prove

runners.name: Lc Tiffen
runners.spotlight: Unexposed; beaten a long way at triple-figure odds on all five starts so far, including on handicap debut (AW); best watched.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way at triple-figure odds on all five starts, including on h'cap debut (AW)

runners.name: Shimmering Spirit
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in maiden/novice company on the AW from 6f-1m; tailed off last on handicap/turf debut (7f, good; 33-1); plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Modest form in novice/maidens on AW; tailed off on turf/handicap debut; plenty to prove

races.race_name: QuinnBet Handicap

runners.name: Laravie
runners.spotlight: Unexposed on the Flat and she made it 3-3 in turf handicaps with an easy Pontefract (2m1f, good) win ten days ago; a slow start there meant she couldn't dominate as she had at Salisbury and Catterick beforehand; 4lb well in under a penalty; obvious chance.
runners.comment: 3-3 in turf handicaps; unexposed, 4lb well in and hard to oppose

runners.name: Betelgeuse
runners.spotlight: Ran away with a Southwell handicap over this trip last October; second in two of his three starts this year (both AW) but he was a bit disappointing over C&D last month; dropped 4lb but he has more to prove on turf.
runners.comment: Good AW runs this year but below par back on turf (C&D) last month; bit to prove

runners.name: Captain Brett
runners.spotlight: C&D win last June remains his only success (also first past post at Bath last July but demoted to second); 4lb higher today and arrives having been well beaten in two starts this year (reared as stalls opened latest).
runners.comment: C&D winner; not at best in two runs this year (reared as stalls opened latest)

races.race_name: Download The QuinnBet App EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Yumuri
runners.spotlight: Last month's debut effort at Goodwood (6f, good) wasn't without hope, but she's up against some well-related newcomers and requires a good leap forward to win.
runners.comment: Goodwood debut wasn't without promise but she takes on some well-related newcomers
runners.owner: Glentree Pastoral Pty Ltd And Partners

runners.name: Dannahue
runners.spotlight: Eleventh foal; closely related to winners Lazuli (5f/6f including 2yo/Group 2; RPR 117) and Indian Springs (7f; 101), half-sister to five winners including Inspiriter (6f including AW 2yo/Listed; 101), Bouquet De Flores (5.5f; 95); dam 5.7f/6f 2yo winner (including Listed; 107); stable's 2yos 2-5 this year; of obvious interest.
runners.comment: Fine pedigree and represents a powerful stable who are 2-5 with 2yos this year; big player

runners.name: Tansy Lane
runners.spotlight: Seventh foal; half-sister to four winners including Echo Point (7f/1m including AW; RPR 106), Mountain Song (1m including Listed; 105) and Western Writer (7f/1m including Listed; 102); dam 7f/1m AW 2yo winner (90), half-sister to 2m Group 3 winner Whispering Gallery; others in today's field bring more precocious pedigrees.
runners.comment: Half-sister to four winners; may find a few of today's rivals proving sharper

runners.name: Blue Flight
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; sister to 5f/6f 2yo winner Spherical (RPR 83), half-sister to winners Surveyor (1m; 92) and The Dunkirk Lads (5f/6f including 2yo/Qatar; 80); dam 7f 2yo winner (89), sister to useful 1m2f winner Inigo Jones; stable's debutantes usually better for a run but they did unleash a winning newcomer at Nottingham last week; check betting.
runners.comment: Her 3 siblings are all winners & looks the part on paper; yard had newcomer win last week

runners.name: Downonmainstreet
runners.spotlight: 65,000gns yearling; ninth foal; half-sister to winners Restive Spirit (1m; RPR 99), Boost (6f/7f AW; 84; dam of 6f Listed winner Benefit) and First Rate (German 8.7f); dam 6f-7f winner (including 6f 2yo Group 1 Cheveley Park; 117); enough paper appeal to warrant a market check.
runners.comment: 65,000gns half-sister to three winners out of a Cheveley Park winner; check betting
runners.owner: Mrs Paul Shanahan & Mrs M V Magnier

runners.name: Musical Force
runners.spotlight: Seventh foal; half-sister to five winners including No Nonsense (6f AW including 2yo; RPR 102), Musical Tribute (6f AW; 89), Musical Angel (6f/7f; 87) and Rich Rhythm (5f turf/7f AW; 77); dam 6f AW 2yo winner (70); stable have had a winning 2yo newcomer this year (well backed).
runners.comment: Half-sister to 5 winners out of 6f AW winner; yard have had a winning 2yo newcomer in 2026

runners.name: Faith In Florence
runners.spotlight: 300,000gns yearling; fifth foal; half-sister to four winners including Oxted (5f-7f including Group 1 King's Stand and July Cup; RPR 123), Chipstead (5f-6f; 110) and Railwayman (6f; 83); dam 5f/6f winner (including 2yo/AW; 96), out of useful 5f winner; bred to sprint and she is well worth a market check.
runners.comment: 300,000gns half-sister to top-class Oxted amongst others; looks the part on paper

runners.name: Spirit Tango
runners.spotlight: 185,000euros foal, 475,000gns yearling; seventh foal; half-sister to winners Majestic Dawn (1m1f-1m3f including Listed; RPR 116), Rousing Encore (Group-placed 5f/6f including 2yo/AW; 99), The Bobster (1m2f AW/1m3f Italian; 61) and Crossbeau (2m3f hurdle); dam unraced; betting should be revealing.
runners.comment: 475,000gns half-sister to four winners (including Listed scorer); betting to guide

runners.name: Enamored
runners.spotlight: 9,000gns foal, 27,000gns yearling; seventh foal; half-sister to six winners including Redemption Time (5f/6f including 2yo/Bahrain; RPR 93), Twilight Romance (6f including 2yo; 93) and Start Me Up (6f; 82); dam 6f-1m winner (including Listed; 105), out of French Oaks winner; had wind op prior to her debut; stable not renowned for winning 2yo newcomers.
runners.comment: 27,000gns half-sister to six winners; had wind op prior to debut; likely best watched

runners.name: Attack Attack
runners.spotlight: Good pedigree but she played up before her Newbury (6.5f, good to firm) debut 17 days ago and ran green in the race itself, eventually beaten over 16l; needs to have learned a lot in a short space of time.
runners.comment: Well beaten at Newbury (6.5f, good to firm) 17 days ago; bred to do much better in time

races.race_name: QuinnBet Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap

runners.name: Law Of Average
runners.spotlight: Enthusiastic front-runner; chasing a hat-trick after wins at Bath (5f, firm) and Hamilton (5f, good to firm); a 6lb rise asks more of him but dangerous to discount.
runners.comment: Enthusiastic front-runner; 5f on quick ground ideal; should make a bold bid for hat-trick

runners.name: Havana Blast
runners.spotlight: Promising 2yo; missed 2025 but he has shown he retains plenty of ability with placed efforts at Wolverhampton (5f) and Kempton (6f) on his last two starts; promise in one run on fast turf; respected.
runners.comment: Missed 2025 but retains a good chunk of ability; best form on AW but unexposed on turf

runners.name: Cressida Wildes
runners.spotlight: Won a Wolverhampton (5f) handicap in February off this mark, but she has beaten just one rival home in three runs since; others much safer.
runners.comment: AW win off this mark in February but beaten only one home in three runs since

runners.name: Michaela's Boy
runners.spotlight: AW win in January and went close 19 days later; unplaced in both turf starts this spring and he needs to raise his game.
runners.comment: On a fair mark but he needs to step up on this year's two turf efforts

runners.name: Twilight Calls
runners.spotlight: Ended a losing run of over four years when the well-backed winner of a 5f handicap at Sandown (good ground; visored first time) last week; still has plenty of handicapping scope given his back class and this should be run to suit him.
runners.comment: Ended long losing run when tried in a visor at Sandown last week; well in under penalty

runners.name: Rocking Ends
runners.spotlight: C&D win last August came off a 3lb higher mark; encouraging reappearance here five weeks ago and today's ground will be more suitable; solid claims dropped into a Class 4.
runners.comment: C&D winner; well treated and now drops in class; one to be interested in

races.race_name: QuinnBet Free Bet For Second To The SP Favourite Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Sibling Rivelry
runners.spotlight: Well backed when winning at Windsor (extended 1m3f, good to firm) last month; the step up to a Class 3 proved beyond her at Goodwood four weeks ago but she is respected back in these calmer waters.
runners.comment: Struggled in a Class 3 latest but her earlier Windsor win brings her right into it

runners.name: Moonjid
runners.spotlight: Ended his 2025 campaign with a breakthrough success at Nottingham (8.3f, good); several solid efforts this year but he still has to conclusively prove he needs this trip.
runners.comment: Running creditably this year but he is still to convince he needs this trip

runners.name: Show Biz Kid
runners.spotlight: Dropped into Class 6 company at Bath (1m2f, firm) last month and proved far too strong for eight rivals; a 7lb rise against better opposition asks more of him but he's still one to consider.
runners.comment: Outclassed rivals in a Class 6 at Bath last month; up 7lb in a better race; respected

runners.name: King's Hand
runners.spotlight: Not straightforward but he won at Lingfield (1m2f, good) last month and backed it up with a good second of four over C&D three weeks ago; should remain competitive.
runners.comment: Not straightforward but he arrives in form and conditions will not faze him

runners.name: Penelope Valentine
runners.spotlight: Her four wins have all been achieved over shorter trips and on AW, but she ran comfortably her best race on turf when second at Nottingham (8.3f, good) 18 days ago; has to prove her stamina and cheekpieces replace the visor today.
runners.comment: Best turf effort 18 days ago (8.3f, good); stamina for 1m2f unproven; change of headgear

runners.name: Charlie's Choice
runners.spotlight: Won three handicaps at Lingfield (1m4f-1m5f, AW) this winter; never a threat when fourth back on turf at Windsor last month; others hold stronger claims.
runners.comment: Better known for AW exploits and could prove vulnerable dropped in trip today

runners.name: Nicator
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo; no improvement for the switch to handicaps when fourth of nine (form not really worked out) at Redcar in April; mark unchanged; needs to raise his game.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo; modest form when 4th on handicap debut in April; still has time to progress

races.race_name: quinnbet.com Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Celestra
runners.spotlight: Boasts a BHA rating of 80 which puts her head and shoulders above her rivals in form terms; on the downside, she has been turned over at long odds-on twice this season and connections now reach for cheekpieces; not one for maximum faith but still an obvious candidate.
runners.comment: Sets a clear standard but beaten at odds-on in both runs this year; headgear now added

runners.name: Summerson
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; sister to 5f/6f 2yo winner Noble Style (including Group 2; RPR 116), half-sister to 1m3f AW winner Little Dorrit (101); dam 6f winner (including 2yo/Listed; 102), out of 5f 2yo Group 2 winner; hooded for debut; betting to guide.
runners.comment: Sister to Group 2 winner Noble Style (RPR 116); hooded for debut; market should guide

runners.name: Topathemorning
runners.spotlight: Promise in two fillies' maiden runs 236 days apart (7f, good to firm and 1m, AW) but likely to be seen to better effect when handicapping.
runners.comment: Some promise in two fillies' maidens but handicaps more suitable after this

runners.name: Blue Hill
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 1m2f winner (RPR 60), half-sister to winners Racer Forever (7f Group 3) and Wrood (1m2f), out of Listed-placed 6f 2yo winner; only of interest if backed.
runners.comment: First foal of a minor 1m2f winner from a good family; only of interest if backed

runners.name: Miss Guinevere
runners.spotlight: Her second run (13 days ago) was better than her debut but she will need another good step forward to come out on top today; place claims.
runners.comment: Second run was better than her debut but she will need further progress to take this

runners.name: Elshie
runners.spotlight: Ran to a modest level in two fillies' maidens (6f/7) in the spring, while leaving the impression she would do much better at some point; by Blue Point but plenty of stamina on the bottom line of her pedigree and stepping up to 1m could unlock some progress.
runners.comment: Can't recommend on bare form of her two runs this spring; 1m could prompt big improvement

runners.name: Esdaile
runners.spotlight: 33-1 and dropped away to be beaten over 12l on her debut here (7f, good to soft) last week; the combination of an extra furlong and faster ground could spark some improvement but it needs to.
runners.comment: Well beaten over 7f here on last week's debut; new trip/ground could spark improvement

runners.name: Delevingne Dream
runners.spotlight: Triple-figure odds when well beaten in two Kempton runs this winter; switch to turf needs to have a transformative effect.
runners.comment: Triple-figure odds when well beaten in two Kempton runs this winter

races.race_name: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Gorgeous Mr George
runners.spotlight: Three wins (7f-1m) and three second-place finishes (6f-1m) at Yarmouth last season; not hit the same heights this time round and although soft ground was a valid excuse last time, he needs a full revival to take this.
runners.comment: Good Yarmouth record in 2025; struggling this year but soft ground an excuse last time

runners.name: Influential
runners.spotlight: Promise over C&D (good to firm) on debut last May; best efforts since have come on AW, including his breakthrough success at Kempton four weeks ago (1m); up in class and back on turf but he does smack of one with a fair bit more to come.
runners.comment: Ran well over C&D on debut; best form since on AW but he is well bred and improving

runners.name: Sea Suite
runners.spotlight: Two wins from five starts this year, the latest in a 7f handicap here (good to firm) three weeks ago; a 2lb rise shouldn't prevent another good run.
runners.comment: Two 7f wins this year, the latest here three weeks ago; 2lb higher but high on the list

runners.name: Knights Gold
runners.spotlight: Had a fine year in 2025 and he spent the spring in the handicapper's claws; offered more encouragement when tried in cheekpieces at Newbury (1m, good to firm) 17 days ago; should have a big run in him.
runners.comment: Handicapper on top this spring but latest Newbury third was more promising; each-way shout

runners.name: Shihoku
runners.spotlight: Proved expensive to follow for James Owen on AW over the winter and he was tailed off back on turf here (1m2f, good) in April; down in the weights but he has a point to prove on his first run for Hayley Burton (same owner).
runners.comment: Expensive to follow for James Owen this winter; down in weights but risky on stable debut

runners.name: Hitched
runners.spotlight: Won twice on turf in the summer of 2023 but better known for his AW exploits since; well beaten at Lingfield (1m1f, good to firm) on last month's return to grass; others are safer.
runners.comment: Has won on turf but rarely seen on it since 2023; others look safer

runners.name: Red Hat Eagle
runners.spotlight: Missed 2025 and it has been tough going in three runs this year; slipping down the weights and likely to come good again soon.
runners.comment: Missed 2025 and it has been tough going so far this year; down in weights; could revive

runners.name: Cancan In The Rain
runners.spotlight: In fine form for Denis Quinn last summer, winning Class 5 handicaps at Southwell and Newmarket in August and running well when fourth of 17 at Ascot 258 days ago; he will need to be cherry ripe to defy this mark back from his absence though.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 in fine form; starts out for new yard after a break with a career-best needed

runners.name: Rogue Encore
runners.spotlight: Third over C&D last June when rated 1lb higher; better form on AW later on in 2025 but he has found it tough going so far in 2026; returns from 11 weeks off with a point to prove.
runners.comment: Third over C&D last June is his best turf form; not at best on AW this year; bit to prove

runners.name: Kalamunda
runners.spotlight: Drew a blank in 2025 after a successful time of things in 2024; returns from nine months off on a dangerous mark but he is a better horse on AW.
runners.comment: Better on AW and he returns from a nine-month break with a point to prove

races.race_name: Irish EBF Auction Series Race (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Sea Of Rain
runners.spotlight: Half-sister by Naval Crown to three winners for the stable; sent off 5-2 favourite for Roscommon debut (7.5f, good to yielding) last month when beaten 2.5l into fourth; that experience can count for a lot in a race of this nature and she's high on the shortlist.
runners.comment: 5-2 favourite for Roscommon debut (7.5f) last month when beaten 2.5l into fourth

runners.name: Bold Move
runners.spotlight: Australia colt; 10,000euros yearling; half-brother to winners See Me Through (1m; RPR 87) and Solar Drive (1m4f Flat, 73; 2m2f hurdle); dam French 9.7f winner, half-sister to 10.5f Listed winner That Which Is Not, out of 10.5f Group 1 winner; bred to get further.
runners.comment: Australia colt; 10,000euros yearling; bred to get further than this

runners.name: Apollo's Comet
runners.spotlight: Mehmas filly holds Group 2 entry; made a satisfactory debut when never-nearer third to Ballydoyle-trained hotpot at Fairyhouse (7f, good) 13 days ago; stable 3-18 with 2yos this year; entitled to improve.
runners.comment: Holds Group 2 entry; third to Ballydoyle-trained hotpot at Fairyhouse; should improve
runners.owner: OMEGA Racing & Seamus McGowan & Niall McInerney

runners.name: Miss Ellie Mae
runners.spotlight: Cost 27,000euros; half-sister to winners Ravenscar (1m6f-2m AW, RPR 85) and Visibility (prolific 7f-1m2f inc AW; 84); dam out of 7f Listed winner; won Naas barrier trial a shade snugly last month; could be more to come.
runners.comment: Dam out of 7f Listed winner; won Naas barrier trial a shade snugly; could be more to come

runners.name: Tosca No Candis
runners.spotlight: Blue Point filly; £40,000 yearling; half-sister to several winners at trips ranging from 5f-1m; dam 5f/7f 2yo winner (93); won Naas barrier trial last month and the third Epilogue went close since on racecourse debut; yard 7-33 with 2yos on these shores this season; warrants respect.
runners.comment: Barrier trial win; yard 7-33 with 2yos here this season so warrants plenty of respect

runners.name: Docks
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; 30,000euros 2yo; closely related to Italian 7.5f/1m 2yo winner Antico Moro, half-brother to winners Tenyatta (7f AW; RPR 54) and Agadir Gold (Czech 1m 2yo/1m3f); dam Italian 1m3f winner; market can guide.
runners.comment: Second runner for yard; 30,000euros 2yo; dam Italian 1m3f winner; market can guide

runners.name: Miss Cornelia
runners.spotlight: Nathaniel filly; 50,000euros yearling; half-sister to German 8.5f winner Cladonia; dam Scandinavian 5.5f-1m winner (inc 2yo; RPR 90), sister to German 1m Group 3 winner Calyxa; runner-up in Naas barrier trial last month, in front of Leadlight; stable's form at present is a concern.
runners.comment: 2nd in Naas barrier trial, in front of Leadlight; stable's form at present is a concern

runners.name: Dakota Jack
runners.spotlight: Ghaiyyath gelding; 62,000euros yearling; dam unraced half-sister to Elysium (7f 2yo Group 3), out of Listed-placed 6f 2yo winning half-sister to Group-placed 5f-7f winner Universo Star; yard 1-21 with 2yos last five seasons.
runners.comment: Ghaiyyath gelding; 62,000euros yearling; yard 1-21 with 2yos last five seasons

runners.name: Matrook
runners.spotlight: New Bay colt; 5,000euros vendor yearling; dam 10.5f-1m6f winner (inc Listed; RPR 106), sister to 1m4f Listed winner Baby Pink, half-sister to smart 1m-1m3f winner Christophermarlowe, out of US 1m4f Grade 3 winner; likely to need the run.
runners.comment: New Bay colt; 5,000euros vendor yearling; dam 10.5f-1m6f winner; may need this

runners.name: Chiquitita Bee
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a single rival home in two outings at Cork and Leopardstown; easily passed over.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a single rival home in two outings at Cork and Leopardstown

runners.name: Leadlight
runners.spotlight: Saxon Warrior colt; 62,000euros yearling; half-brother to five winners inc Total Commitment (6f/7f inc 2yo/AW; RPR 101), Crystallium (7f 2yo inc AW; 95) and Shimmering (1m AW 2yo; 94); dam out of 1m 2yo Group 1 winner; fourth in Naas barrier trial last month (Miss Cornelia second); in the mix.
runners.comment: 62,000euros yearling; half-brother to 5 winners; fourth in Naas barrier trial last month

races.race_name: BOYLE Sports Money Back If 2nd To SP Fav Handicap

runners.name: Goal Exceeded
runners.spotlight: Two wins include over this trip at the Curragh over a year ago off 73; fine C&D runner-up on comeback but race may have come too soon at the Curragh four days later; solid third at Cork on latest and handles any ground; remains nicely treated.
runners.comment: C&D form; solid third at Cork on latest and handles any ground; remains nicely treated
runners.owner: Ms H McGuinness/Martin W White/Declan Sheridan/Breen White

runners.name: Reposado
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer finished runner-up in two 6f large-field Curragh handicaps in April/May but failed to cope with the drop in trip in 5f Dundalk maiden last month; should be competitive back over a more suitable trip.
runners.comment: 2nd in 2 6f large-field Curragh handicaps in April/May; failed to handle drop in trip lto
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Irish Rumour
runners.spotlight: Only one win from 30 starts (5f); went close on occasions last year; far too keen on seasonal return at Roscommon last month but much-improved second of 20 at Cork 15 days ago (6.5f, yielding), best work at the finish; in the mix.
runners.comment: Much-improved second of 20 at Cork 15 days ago (6.5f, yielding), best work at the finish

runners.name: Hexagonal
runners.spotlight: Maiden with little enough mileage on the clock; creditable efforts in handicaps of late but will do well to get an easy run to the front from his draw here; more needed.
runners.comment: Solid in handicaps of late but will do well to get an easy run to the front from his draw

runners.name: Send Harry
runners.spotlight: Dependable on turf last summer, making the frame on multiple occasions before scoring at Dundalk (7f) in September; will need to step up on the form of this year's efforts at Cork and the Curragh.
runners.comment: Made the frame on turf several times last season; AW win; needs to improve currently

runners.name: Phoenix Pairc
runners.spotlight: Gained first win in 7f Dundalk maiden in October; never counted in two AW handicaps at the end of 2025; ran well in turf maidens last year so can't be discounted but fitness has to be taken on trust after a layoff.
runners.comment: Ran well in turf maidens last year but fitness has to be taken on trust after a layoff

runners.name: Faoladh
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a good spell last summer, including a win at this track (1m, good); fair efforts in defeat this year before improving to score at Down Royal (7f, good) on latest; up 5lb but right in the mix again as long as the ground doesn't deteriorate.
runners.comment: Course winner; good win at Dowl Royal last time; up 5lb; good chance if ground stays dry

runners.name: Loingseoir
runners.spotlight: Largely consistent veteran, 7-76 in his career and made the frame on all four visits to this track, including a week ago when beaten 2.75l fourth behind a progressive winner; needs things to fall right given how he's ridden; drawn wide; chance if granted a solid pace to aim at.
runners.comment: Always runs well at this track; needs things to fall right given how he's ridden; chance
runners.owner: W Keeling & M C Grassick & Mrs M J Grassick

runners.name: Mickey The Steel
runners.spotlight: Has been running well in defeat this term, including a second of 23 over this trip at the Curragh in March; wasn't beaten far when seventh of 21 at Navan 11 days ago (5f); acts on good but best on soft/heavy.
runners.comment: Has been running well in defeat this term; acts on good but best on soft/heavy

runners.name: Eichan San
runners.spotlight: Now 0-10 but has ability judged on his keeping-on fourth of 21 at the Curragh in April; didn't get a clear run at Down Royal last month and completely missed the break when fifth of 20 at Cork 15 days ago (Irish Rumour second); good chance if he can get away on terms.
runners.comment: Some hard luck stories of late; good chance if he can get away on terms

runners.name: Apache Outlaw
runners.spotlight: Last win was in late 2024, but was second over this trip at last year's Galway festival and some good AW runs over the winter; not disgraced at Listowel 18 days ago; has much lower mark on turf.
runners.comment: Second over this trip at last year's Galway festival; has much lower mark on turf

runners.name: Ardeur
runners.spotlight: Won 6f maiden for Ralph Beckett and later some decent efforts in defeat in France up to 1m2f; hasn't had her preferred decent ground for current connections so potential improver if it comes up good.
runners.comment: Hasn't had her preferred ground for connections so potential improver if it comes up good

runners.name: Navagio
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner but losing run stretches back to July 2023; recent Roscommon sixth was mildly encouraging but failed to back it up at the Curragh; plenty more needed now.
runners.comment: Three-time winner but losing run stretches back to July 2023; plenty more needed now

runners.name: Perfect Base
runners.spotlight: Best of maiden efforts came over this sort of trip on good ground; disqualified after placing fourth at Down Royal when rider failed to weigh in; blinkers go on now for handicap debut after an absence; market can guide.
runners.comment: Disqualified (4th) at Down Royal; makes handicap debut in blinkers after absence

runners.name: Caffu Zafeen
runners.spotlight: Placed over 6f/7f in Britain two seasons ago; never a factor on 5f Navan stable debut 11 days ago after a year off the track; market will reveal expectations.
runners.comment: Never a factor on 5f Navan stable debut 11 days ago after a year off the track

runners.name: Free Solo
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner, including here as 2yo; ran well here twice last summer (runner-up in this race); out of form this term before eyecatching sixth over C&D last week after a slow start, two places behind Loingseoir; each-way possibilities.
runners.comment: Sixth over C&D last week after a slow start, two places behind Loingseoir; each-way chance

runners.name: Buttermere Ridge
runners.spotlight: Nothing of any note in his form since early in his career; off the track 234 days; new headgear combination.
runners.comment: Nothing of any note in his form since early in his career; off the track 234 days

races.race_name: Bulmers Live Handicap

runners.name: Battle Borne
runners.spotlight: Won modest 7f Dundalk maiden in February and best run since when runner-up in 1m claimer there last month; tailed off in two turf handicaps prior and is easy to oppose here.
runners.comment: Tailed off in two turf handicaps prior to AW claimer second and is easy to oppose here

runners.name: Bucaneer's Spirit
runners.spotlight: Four wins at around this trip, most recently off 60 at Cork in April; decent third at Roscommon last month and filled the same spot at Limerick eight days ago; one to consider again.
runners.comment: Four wins at around this trip, most recently off 60 at Cork in April; in good form

runners.name: Bay Of Dreams
runners.spotlight: Won twice for James Ferguson last year, including on turf at Nottingham; tailed off first two starts for this yard but beaten only 3.5l when ninth at Down Royal latest (7f, good; raced keenly), offering something to build on.
runners.comment: Beaten only 3.5l when ninth at Down Royal on latest and that offers something to build on

runners.name: Running Point
runners.spotlight: Promise in three turf maidens last year at 6.5f-7f on good and yielding ground; never a factor on seasonal/handicap debut at Roscommon (7.5f); headgear combination is now tried; market can guide.
runners.comment: Never a factor on seasonal/handicap debut at Roscommon (7.5f); headgear combination

runners.name: Dawn Flame
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; Dundalk win in January (1m) seems to have been a flash in the pan; started his career with two poor runs on turf and no better at the Curragh 15 days ago.
runners.comment: AW winner; started his career with two poor runs on turf and no better at the Curragh

runners.name: Local Lad
runners.spotlight: Couple of good runs in quality 2yo contests but didn't match that level in five runs last season and struggled on seasonal/handicap debut at the Curragh in April.
runners.comment: Couple of good runs in quality 2yo contests but didn't match that level in a while

runners.name: Zaraahmando
runners.spotlight: Three wins last season at 7f-1m on good and soft ground, going up a total of 21lb; good return fourth at Naas (7f, soft); below-par next time at Roscommon but kept on well when sixth of 19 at the Curragh (1m, good) on latest; fitting of tongue-tie may bring on improvement.
runners.comment: Progressive last season and ran well at the Curragh last time; tongue-tie can improve him

runners.name: Cosmic Funk
runners.spotlight: Minor promise in maidens but excellent effort on handicap debut over C&D (soft to heavy) a fortnight ago when second to an easy winner; the draw hasn't been as kind this time around but respected.
runners.comment: Excellent effort on handicap debut over C&D (soft to heavy) a fortnight ago; chance

runners.name: Smithfield
runners.spotlight: Reappeared in April with best handicap effort (well-backed) yet in finishing fourth at Limerick (1m, yielding), staying on; didn't appear to stay 1m2f when tailed off at Fairyhouse three weeks ago; cheekpieces; wide draw.
runners.comment: Reappeared in April with best handicap effort (well-backed); amiss last time; wide draw

runners.name: Go Out
runners.spotlight: Maiden has done most of his racing on the AW, runner-up three times; fair turf form this term and excuses at Roscommon on latest (raced freely and saddle slipped); needs to find extra but could go well.
runners.comment: Fair turf form this term and excuses at Roscommon on latest; could go well
runners.owner: Mrs John Murphy & John Pearson & Karl Kirwan

runners.name: Fleetfootsoldier
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-13 on turf (4-18 on AW); looked in need of the run on stable debut at Listowel 18 days ago; others preferred.
runners.comment: Looked in need of the run on stable debut at Listowel 18 days ago; others preferred

runners.name: Ocean Echo
runners.spotlight: Promise in maidens, runner-up at Cork last month (7f, soft to heavy) before good fourth of 14 at Fairyhouse (7f, good) 13 days ago under positive tactics; looks well treated in receipt of the 3yo allowance on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Promise in maidens; looks well treated in receipt of the 3yo allowance on handicap debut
runners.owner: R Scahill, S Begley, J & C Bourke

runners.name: Millhone
runners.spotlight: Didn't lack market support but ran no sort of race after blowing the start on seasonal return at Gowran; a lot better with midfield finish at Fairyhouse three weeks ago but races from 11lb out of the handicap here; cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Better with midfield finish at Fairyhouse but races from 11lb out of the handicap
runners.owner: N J Hitchins & Mrs John M Weld

runners.name: Heliogabalus
runners.spotlight: Opened his account at the 11th attempt when dead-heating at the Galway festival last year; met trouble in running at the Curragh next time; never a factor at Listowel; off 269 days; may need the run.
runners.comment: Dead-heated at the Galway festival last year; two poor runs after; absence to overcome

runners.name: Sayfa Fad
runners.spotlight: Stayed on well to land Bellewstown handicap (1m, good) last August on stable debut; never landed a blow in two starts after; 264 days off.
runners.comment: Won at Bellewstown on stable debut last August; poor next two starts; absence

runners.name: Perfect Judgement
runners.spotlight: Four AW wins; sole turf win came at this course (7f); didn't count first two starts for this stable but failed to get a clear run late on when seventh of 15 at Limerick (1m) eight days ago (Bucaneer's Spirit third); interesting off career-low mark.
runners.comment: Didn't get clear run at Limerick eight days ago; interesting off career-low mark

runners.name: Verhoyen
runners.spotlight: Veteran is an eight-time winner, with the last of those at Fairyhouse in 2024; ran a good race from a wide draw at that venue on penultimate but not as good at Limerick eight days ago; handicapped to go close if running to his ability.
runners.comment: Veteran is an eight-time winner; handicapped to go close if he can run to his ability
runners.owner: Paul Cullen & Joseph E Keeling & M C Grassick

runners.name: Navajo Point
runners.spotlight: Quiet in maidens; some promise on handicap debut at Cork (7f) but completely tailed off next time at Roscommon; lengthy absence to overcome.
runners.comment: Some promise on handicap debut at Cork (7f) but completely tailed off next time; absence

races.race_name: BOYLE Sports Home Of The Early Payout Rated Race

runners.name: Johnny Soda
runners.spotlight: Cork handicap winner over this trip on soft; twice unplaced over longer trips since then; others have more persuasive claims.
runners.comment: Cork handicap winner over this trip on soft, twice unplaced over longer trips since then

runners.name: Sir Benji
runners.spotlight: Did well to win first time out, coping with heavy ground in a 1m2f Navan event, despite showing his inexperience; well beaten in third behind a useful sort at Roscommon; perhaps not guaranteed to show his best form now that he is dropped in trip.
runners.comment: Debut winner on heavy, both runs over further, may find this trip inadequate

runners.name: Halon Bay
runners.spotlight: Made debut over 1m2f; showed improvement over 1m at Bellewstown but soundly beaten in fourth; narrow winner of a 7f course maiden last month; may find a few too good now.
runners.comment: Narrow winner of a 7f course maiden last month, may find a few too good now

runners.name: Red Autumn
runners.spotlight: Won 1m Bellewstown maiden last year and decent second in Roscommon winners' contest; encouraging comeback run in 7f Madrid Handicap at Naas in late March, staying on late into fourth; not at good over 1m1f here a fortnight ago.
runners.comment: Fair fourth in 7f Madrid Handicap in March, not at good over 1m1f here a fortnight ago

runners.name: Glitter And Glory
runners.spotlight: Only poor run at two was when out of her depth in a valuable sales race; consistent performer at Dundalk; possibly more effective on AW but not beaten far in fifth in a handicap over 7f at the Curragh on latest.
runners.comment: Possibly more effective at Dundalk but not beaten far over 7f at the Curragh on latest

runners.name: Shaftesbury Avenue
runners.spotlight: First two runs were over 1m on heavy ground; 5l third of 23 on debut at the Curragh behind Victory Tip; won at Navan in first-time cheekpieces, beating a subsequent winner; first-time blinkers may help him to bounce back from a disappointing Naas run, trainer/rider won this last year.
runners.comment: May bounce back from a disappointing Naas run, trainer/rider won this last year, blinkered
runners.owner: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg

runners.name: Krasimir
runners.spotlight: Placed on all four starts; went closest last year when a neck second in a 17-runner 6f maiden at Naas; made a lot of the running over 7f at Gowran on final start at two; met some interference when second over 7f at Fairyhouse on reappearance; tackles this trip for the first time; holds a definite chance.
runners.comment: Placed on all four starts, tackles this trip for the first time, holds a definite chance

runners.name: God Of Power
runners.spotlight: Allocated a mark of 80 after 7f maiden win at Limerick; now up to 88 as a result of finishing second to a 91-rated older horse at Listowel; appeals as a progressive type.
runners.comment: Improved on the form of a Limerick maiden win when second at Listowel, could feature

runners.name: Golddream
runners.spotlight: Won over this trip on AW on second start; soundly beaten in third over an extended 1m2f at Naas early last month on turf debut.
runners.comment: Won over this trip on AW on second start, soundly beaten over a longer trip on turf debut

races.race_name: 1878 At 3Arena Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Chipsrdown
runners.spotlight: Failed to build on a positive handicap debut when well beaten at Killarney; much better with a visor on her two most recent starts at Gowran, placed both times; fair chance of reaching the money again.
runners.comment: Has been placed at Gowran on her two most recent starts, fair chance of reaching money

runners.name: Half Nutz
runners.spotlight: Six wins to his name but on a losing run that stretches back to September 2022; not beaten far in two of four visits to Gowran in recent months, and last week's Limerick third offers some hope.
runners.comment: The most recent of his six wins was in 2022, last week's Limerick third offers some hope

runners.name: Ella's Gold
runners.spotlight: Twice second in nurseries last season; appeared not to stay 1m2f at Fairyhouse on seasonal debut, or may simply have needed the run; holds a leading chance on last week's C&D second; cheekpieces added now.
runners.comment: Twice second in nurseries, big chance on last week's C&D second, cheekpieces added now

runners.name: Limestone Red
runners.spotlight: Has won twice on AW; most recent success in January, 0-14 record on turf is a negative, but worth bearing in mind that his rating in this sphere is 15lb lower than his current AW mark.
runners.comment: Has won twice on AW, most recent success in January, 0-14 record on turf is a negative

runners.name: Nephin Mountain
runners.spotlight: Best form over 7f on soft at Gowran; finished in midfield here last week, held by Ella's Gold on that running.
runners.comment: Best form over 7f on soft at Gowran, midfield here last week, held by Ella's Gold

runners.name: Lust
runners.spotlight: Has come down in the world a long way since a 7f Listed win for Ger Lyons in 2022; rated 102 at his peak; was running off a career-low 53 when starting the season with an encouraging third in a 1m2f event at Fairyhouse 13 days ago; good chance if building on that.
runners.comment: Has come down in the world since a Listed win in 2022, fair chance after seasonal debut

runners.name: Smooth
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden who has shown nothing of much note in handicaps; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Nine-race maiden who has shown nothing of much note in handicaps, hard to fancy

runners.name: Shanlieve
runners.spotlight: Some promise in 3yo maidens on good ground; however, he has beaten only one rival in each of his two handicap attempts; seems safe to rule out against that background.
runners.comment: Has beaten only one rival in each of his two handicap attempts, seems safe to rule out

runners.name: Akashaa
runners.spotlight: Lacks a recent run but is relatively unexposed; can be given a chance on her second over 1m on AW at Dundalk last August.
runners.comment: Lacks a recent run but can be given a chance on her second over 1m on AW last August

runners.name: Jewels Legacy
runners.spotlight: Weak form; 4lb out of the handicap, big improvement needed in first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Weak form, 4lb out of the handicap, big improvement needed in first-time blinkers

runners.name: Pinball Wizard
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner in Britain; only glimpses of peak form since second in a claimer early last season; not far behind Half Nutz when seventh of 16 over 1m at Gowran on seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Six-time winner in Britain, only glimpses of peak form since second in a claimer last year

runners.name: Curious Jane
runners.spotlight: Has beaten very few rivals in maidens, stable's solid recent form is the only positive.
runners.comment: Has beaten very few rivals in maidens, stable's solid recent form is the only positive

runners.name: Hieloray
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with a promising second at Gowran but was beaten 7l and the going was heavy; trainer has had two recent wins with a handicap hurdler.
runners.comment: Ended last season with a good second at Gowran but was beaten 7l and the going was heavy

runners.name: Lohengrin
runners.spotlight: Won 7f Laytown maiden last September; poor winter form at Dundalk; brought down at Bellewstown on return; does not appeal on the evidence of his last three starts.
runners.comment: Won a 7f Laytown maiden last September, makes no appeal on this season's form

runners.name: Rampage
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner, last turf win in 2023 over this trip at Galway on soft; two AW wins since; unplaced in five outings since March; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Four wins, last turf success in 2023 at Galway on soft; two AW wins since; hard to fancy
runners.owner: Mrs P McCourt/Whyte's Stamullen Syndicate/S Sweeney

runners.name: William F Browne
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner for Ger Lyons in 2024; best run for this yard was when second in a 16-runner contest at last year's Galway festival; unplaced in his last six races on the Flat; ran out at the first in a maiden hurdle at Clonmel last time.
runners.comment: Won twice for Ger Lyons in 2024, best run for this yard was at last year's Galway festival

runners.name: Max Meridius
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with a respectable fifth on handicap debut in a 25-runner 1m2f Curragh race on heavy ground; fit from hurdling; could go well.
runners.comment: Fit from hurdling, ended last season with a respectable fifth in a 25-runner Curragh race

runners.name: Genuine Jim
runners.spotlight: Both wins on soft, including over 1m at Cork early last season; ended the campaign with a respectable run at the Curragh; three poor runs this term; needs to show signs of a return to form.
runners.comment: Both wins on soft including over 1m at Cork last year, three poor runs this term

races.race_name: 1878 At 3Arena Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Queen Aethelflaed
runners.spotlight: Made a low-key start to the season before coming good on last two starts; second to Real Petite at Gowran before relegating that rival to sixth at the same venue last Friday; carries a 7lb penalty here, with Orla Tynan again claiming 7lb.
runners.comment: Made a low-key start to the season before coming good on last two starts, 7lb penalty

runners.name: Borora Aura
runners.spotlight: Third placing in a Ballinrobe claimer was a step in the right direction; needs to step up from her handicap debut at Gowran.
runners.comment: Third in a claimer last month, needs to step up from her handicap debut at Gowran

runners.name: Staywitherjohnny
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden, placed at Gowran three starts back; closely matched with Nibras Rainbow on running over C&D last week; place possibility.
runners.comment: Closely matched with Nibras Rainbow on running over C&D last week, place possibility

runners.name: Desert Of The Sea
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after 17 starts, placed four times; had a good spell on AW early in the year; not without a chance with a capable 7lb claimer abaard.
runners.comment: A maiden after 17 starts, had a good spell on AW early in the year, capable 7lb claimer

runners.name: Famous Enough
runners.spotlight: Went close at Dundalk in April; unplaced at Gowran since; turf record is unconvincing, 1-20, placed only once.
runners.comment: Went close at Dundalk in April, unplaced at Gowran since, turf record is unconvincing

runners.name: Nibras Rainbow
runners.spotlight: Followed a poor effort at Cork with a 33-1 win at Killarney (1m, good) last month, holding on by a short head after making all; backed that up with a C&D third last week, only a nose behind Ella's Gold who bids to advertise the form in the first heat; makes plenty of appeal.
runners.comment: Backed up last month's long-priced Killarney win with a C&D third, one for the shortlist

runners.name: Shakita
runners.spotlight: Carried out early in a hurdle race last time; previously in rear in a C&D handicap at 50-1; transformation needed.
runners.comment: Carried out early in a hurdle race last time, previously in rear in a C&D handicap at 50-1

runners.name: Yquem
runners.spotlight: Promise in a couple of AW runs for John O'Donoghue; weaker form in five runs on turf; may improve from her recent stable debut at Fairyhouse.
runners.comment: Promise in a couple of AW runs for John O'Donoghue; weaker form in five runs on turf

runners.name: Medieval Night
runners.spotlight: By far his best run was when second over C&D last August on handicap debut; modest 2026 form; eighth of 16 behind Real Petite at Gowran last time; tongue-tie now added to cheekpieces.
runners.comment: By far his best run when second over C&D last August on handicap debut, modest 2026 form

runners.name: Sarmiento Power
runners.spotlight: 1m4f AW winner; decent form at Dundalk last autumn, poor at that venue this year; same story at Navan on latest.
runners.comment: Decent form at Dundalk last autumn, poor at that venue this year, same at Navan on latest

runners.name: Real Petite
runners.spotlight: Showed very little in 12 starts before improving to land a Gowran handicap at 50-1, beating Queen Aethelflaed by 2.25l; beaten 4l in sixth when that rival reversed the form at the same venue last week.
runners.comment: Beat Queen Aethelflaed at Gowran early in the month, that rival reversed form last week

runners.name: Private Larry
runners.spotlight: Weak maiden form; has been soundly beaten in handicap runs this season.
runners.comment: Weak maiden form, has been soundly beaten in two handicap runs this season

runners.name: Deuteronomy
runners.spotlight: C&D winner 12 months ago; long-priced Cork winner last month, his fourth success in a 41-race career; failed to feature at Fairyhouse 13 days ago after a slow start.
runners.comment: Long-priced Cork winner last month, failed to feature at Fairyhouse after a slow start

runners.name: Rockbury Lad
runners.spotlight: Ended winter AW campaign with two placed efforts; down the field twice at this venue since then; held by Nibras Rainbow and Staywitherjohnny on last week's form.
runners.comment: Ended winter AW campaign with two placed efforts, down the field twice at this venue since
runners.owner: P E Ryan & J G Shanahan

runners.name: Virgil Tibbs
runners.spotlight: Shaped quite well at Galway last August on third maiden start; not a great deal of promise in handicaps, not in the first six in four attempts.
runners.comment: Shaped quite well at Galway on third maiden start, not much promise in handicaps

runners.name: Crypto Crash
runners.spotlight: Best effort when second over 1m4f on fast ground for David Marnane in 2024; unplaced in six runs over hurdles since his last Flat start.
runners.comment: Unplaced in six runs over hurdles since his last Flat start, best form was in 2024

runners.name: Sheamus Seimhiu
runners.spotlight: Never closer than sixth in nine starts; lacks a recent outing, hard to make a case for him.
runners.comment: Never closer than sixth in nine starts, lacks a recent outing, hard to make a case for him

races.race_name: 1878 At 3Arena Maiden

runners.name: Eniac
runners.spotlight: Opening course handicap effort (1m4f, good) offered plenty of encouragement, coming home strongly after not clear run to finish fourth; sent off favourite at Down Royal but never a factor (10.5f); considered back in a maiden over a more suitable trip.
runners.comment: Good chance judged on course handicap run on penultimate; chance back in a maiden

runners.name: Changing Lanes
runners.spotlight: Winning potential in both starts; beaten 1.75l on debut at Dundalk (1m4f); neck runner-up at Roscommon last month (10.5f, good to yielding); leading chance back up in trip.
runners.comment: Winning potential in both starts, neck runner-up at Roscommon last month; big chance

runners.name: Kashala
runners.spotlight: Nicely-bred and caught the eye on debut 15 days ago when never-nearer fourth of nine at the Curragh (1m4f, yielding) after a slow start; that looked a hot maiden and she won't have to improve a great deal to get involved here.
runners.comment: Caught the eye on debut over at the Curragh; won't need to improve much to play a part
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Echo Inferno
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; Too Darn Hot gelding cost 200,000gns as a yearling; soon beaten on sole 2yo outing at Tipperary last August; gelded over the winter; fair fifth of 15 at the Curragh on return (1m2f, good; beaten 2l); likely improver.
runners.comment: Gelded; fair fifth of 15 at the Curragh on return (1m2f, good; beaten 2l); likely improver

runners.name: Essential Comedy
runners.spotlight: Not sold 55,000euros 2yo; dam 6f 2yo winner (inc French Group 3; RPR 101), out of 1m 2yo winning half-sister to useful 7f-1m2f winner King Charles and useful Flat stayer/2m-2m4f hurdle winner Ghimaar; can only watch.
runners.comment: Not sold 55,000euros 2yo; dam 6f 2yo winner (inc French Group 3; RPR 101); watch

runners.name: Chairmanforlife
runners.spotlight: Speedily-bred gelding didn't count on 1m2f course debut and didn't see out this trip when beaten 11l at Ballinrobe on latest; looks the yard's second string on jockey arrangements.
runners.comment: Speedily-bred; didn't see out this trip at Ballinrobe; stable's second string

runners.name: Phlox
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; half-sister to winners Maxux (1m1f/9.4f inc Group 3; RPR 114), Cunco (6f 2yo/1m2f Group 3; 105), Firth Of Clyde (1m AW; 89) and Cassini (1m2f; 88); dam 7f-1m2f winner (inc 2yo/Group 3; 110); any market support could be significant.
runners.comment: Half-sister to some classy winners; any market support could be significant
runners.owner: Barnane Stud & Hollywood Stud Ireland Limited

runners.name: Quick Return
runners.spotlight: Invincible Spirit gelding; 10,000euros foal; dam placed 7f/10.5f AW (RPR 67), half-sister to numerous winners inc Spin Cycle (5f Listed), Tucuman (1m1f/1m2f Listed), San Sichara (7f Group 3) and Spianacre (7f 2yo Listed); best watched.
runners.comment: 10,000euros foal; dam placed 7f/10.5f AW (RPR 67), half-sister to numerous winners; watch

runners.name: Stormy Days
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; half-brother to 1m4f winner Blondina (RPR 63); dam 1m1f/1m2f winner (97), closely related to 6.5f-1m winner Wexford Opera; tongue-tied for debut.
runners.comment: Half-brother to 1m4f winner Blondina (RPR 63); tongue-tied for debut

runners.name: Sakoku
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; Sea The Stars filly was sent off 15-2 for Curragh debut when always in rear (1m2f, good; Echo Inferno finished fifth); still early days and could improve now tongue-tied.
runners.comment: Poor debut effort; still early days and could improve now tongue-tied

runners.name: Parish Team
runners.spotlight: Home-bred Parish Hall gelding; sent off at 22-1 when midfield on Gowran debut (9.5f, yielding) 17 days ago; improvement needed.
runners.comment: Sent off at 22-1 when midfield on Gowran debut (9.5f, yielding) 17 days ago

runners.name: Solar Jet
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in the Roscommon maiden where Changing Lanes finished second; safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Tailed off in the Roscommon maiden where Changing Lanes finished second; safe to rule out

races.race_name: BOYLE Sports Squad Builder (Q.R.) Maiden

runners.name: Joe Cool
runners.spotlight: Placed in three of his four Flat starts; steps up in trip after seconds at the Curragh and Bellewstown this term; leading chance now after a maiden hurdle success achieved at the main expense of a Gordon Elliott-trained runner who was a Grade 3 AQPS winner last year; form of Bellewstown race shown in a good light by the winner Tim Toe when third in Tuesday's Ascot Stakes.
runners.comment: Placed in three of his four Flat starts, leading chance now after a maiden hurdle success

runners.name: Gaoth Chuil
runners.spotlight: Useful mare, winning a bumper and three times over hurdles at up to 3m; recent chase form not quite at the same level; fourth behind A Dream To Share in this race 12 months ago; found 1m4f too short at Cork.
runners.comment: Bumper winner, useful hurdler, fourth in this race last year, found 1m4f too short at Cork

runners.name: Salsinha
runners.spotlight: Won a good-ground bumper at Cork last summer on debut, with Beir Bua back in fourth; good standard in five starts over hurdles, including third in a Listed contest at Thurles last December; chased home the useful Switch From Diesel at Punchestown before beating Fraher Field readily at Listowel; strong contender.
runners.comment: Bumper winner on good, beat Fraher Field in a maiden hurdle at Listowel, strong contender

runners.name: The Banger Doyle
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner in 2020; best known as a three-time chase scorer, runner-up five times on the Flat (three times at Killarney, twice at Tramore); vulnerable in a race of this type at the age of ten.
runners.comment: Bumper winner and three-time chase scorer, runner-up five times on the Flat, vulnerable

runners.name: Fraher Field
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner last August after several good runs in defeat; improved on previous hurdles form when second to Salsinha at Listowel; yard won this last year with John Gleeson aboard; significant that he partners Salsinha here.
runners.comment: Bumper winner, improved on previous hurdles form when second to Salsinha at Listowel
runners.owner: John J Flynn, Pierce Flynn, Conor Lannen

runners.name: Kaleo Ametsa
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form in bumpers; some promise at Clonmel on her most recent hurdles start; other are more appealing.
runners.comment: Ordinary form in bumpers, some promise on her most recent hurdles start

runners.name: Mart Lane
runners.spotlight: Fair form over hurdles in the final months of last season; second in a maiden at Wexford and fourth in a 21-runner handicap at Fairyhouse despite not jumping well; has a useful Flat pedigree, potentially interesting.
runners.comment: Fair form over hurdles in the final months of last season, has a useful Flat pedigree

runners.name: Kimy
runners.spotlight: Has won only one of her 25 races over hurdles, placed six times, currently rated 95; makes Flat debut in the company of several recent hurdle winners who are less exposed; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Has won only one of her 25 races over hurdles, placed six times, Flat debut, hard to fancy

runners.name: Certamen
runners.spotlight: Fourth in a bumper at Thurles on debut at four; only mild promise over hurdles; not a likely contender in this company.
runners.comment: Fourth in a bumper, mild promise over hurdles, not a likely contender in this company

runners.name: Beir Bua
runners.spotlight: Won a Wexford bumper last August and was second on hurdling debut at Kilbeggan later that month; confirmed a liking for good ground with a wide-margin maiden hurdle win at Sligo last month; fair chance, though held by Salsinha on the form of a bumper clash at Cork last year.
runners.comment: Bumper winner, has confirmed liking for good ground with a maiden hurdle win, fair chance

runners.name: Golden Fascination
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; sister to Tony Martin-trained 1m5f turf/2m AW winner Elle Dorado Rock; dam 1m 2yo winner, half-sister to Group-placed middle-distance winner Majeed.
runners.comment: Sister to 1m5f turf/2m AW winner Elle Dorado Rock; dam 1m 2yo winner by Paco Boy

runners.name: Lauro's Legend
runners.spotlight: Placed over hurdles on good ground at Wexford last summer; pulled up in two handicap hurdle outings this year.
runners.comment: Placed over hurdles last summer, pulled up in two handicap hurdle outings this year

runners.name: Donnrua Dream
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner over hurdles, currently rated a modest 93; has been chasing; up against it on Flat debut as a 9yo.
runners.comment: Three-time winner over hurdles, has been chasing; up against it on Flat debut as a 9yo

runners.name: Eagle Fang
runners.spotlight: Very useful staying handicap hurdler, rated 145, has won twice and was runner-up in valuable handicaps at Aintree and Ascot in recent months; also a winner over fences; had one Flat run in 2023; may find a few too quick for him over this trip.
runners.comment: Very useful staying handicap hurdler, also a winner over fences, had one Flat run in 2023

runners.name: Chain Of Destiny
runners.spotlight: Nothing of note in his form since a long-priced third in a 1m Curragh maiden in 2022; missed two full seasons before returning last month.
runners.comment: Nothing of note in his form since a long-priced third in a 1m Curragh maiden in 2022

runners.name: Faerie Tales
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a single rival in two runs at two; missed last season; safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a single rival in two runs at two, missed last season, safe to rule out

runners.name: Must Meet Cecil
runners.spotlight: Five-time winning hurdler; not at his best for some time; well beaten in two Flat maidens this term, including Bellewstown event in which Joe Cool was second.
runners.comment: Five wins over hurdles, not at best recently, well beaten in two Flat maidens this term

runners.name: Wonderwalk
runners.spotlight: Very modest Flat form; not a bad run on the first of three hurdle starts; up against it.
runners.comment: Very modest Flat form, not a bad run on the first of three hurdle starts, up against it

races.race_name: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Oh Yes You Do
runners.spotlight: Won a Leicester maiden (1m, good to soft) last autumn and she's run respectably in handicaps at Doncaster (1m2f, good to firm) and Windsor (11.4f, good) this spring; still unexposed but she needs to find more to get back on the scoresheet.
runners.comment: Two respectable efforts in handicaps this spring but she needs to find something extra

runners.name: Blue Noon
runners.spotlight: Showed clear promise in novice/maiden events but was 14-1 on handicap debut at Chester (10.3f, good) last month and she finished a well-held sixth of ten; now drops back in grade but this is competitive and she needs improvement.
runners.comment: Still early days but this is competitive and she needs improvement

runners.name: Alice De Clare
runners.spotlight: Progressive filly who made it 2-3 in handicaps when completing a double at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm) three weeks ago; this is tougher off 4lb higher but she's on a roll and could continue on an upward curve; key player.
runners.comment: Improver who made it 2-3 in handicaps when scoring at Leicester (1m2f); key player

runners.name: Windbreaker
runners.spotlight: Showed promise behind smart rivals in his two juvenile runs but was only third of six when favourite for a Chepstow maiden (1m2f, good to soft) on his reappearance last month; has bit to prove after that but the slow ground may have been an issue and he could have a good future in handicaps.
runners.comment: No real progress in three qualifying runs but he could have a good future in handicaps

runners.name: Light Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Went close on her Kempton debut (7f, AW) in April; well held in next two runs but she kicked off her handicap career with a respectable second at Hamilton (8.3f, good to firm) last month; this is a much deeper race but she rallied well last time and is not ruled out back up in trip.
runners.comment: Mixed form so far but she still has potential and is not ruled out back up in trip

runners.name: Mostaan
runners.spotlight: Beaten 7l when fifth on his first two starts but he found improvement when fifth of 12 in a Southwell maiden (1m, AW) last month; looks on a fair mark now switched to a handicap and he's open to more progress on this step up to 1m2f; respected.
runners.comment: Promising fifth in a 1m maiden (AW) last time and he needs a close look on handicap debut

runners.name: Last Verse
runners.spotlight: 75,000euros son of Camelot; showed ability in his first two juvenile runs (7f) and he had an excuse on heavy ground in final start last season; gelded since and he should have more to offer in middle-distance handicaps this year; one to keep an eye on back from a break.
runners.comment: Unexposed gelding and he should have more to offer in middle-distance handicaps this year

runners.name: Antiquarian
runners.spotlight: Struggled at big prices in three qualifying runs including on AW here (1m2f) two weeks ago; now goes handicapping but opening mark looks on the high side and she needs major improvement.
runners.comment: Struggled in maiden/novice events and she needs major improvement on handicap debut

runners.name: Molo Del Palazzo
runners.spotlight: Palace Pier filly who is out of an unraced sister to US 1m6f Listed winner Cedar Mountain; shaped with promise behind some useful rivals in her qualifying runs and she's an interesting contender now upped to 1m2f on handicap debut; needs a close look in the market.
runners.comment: Unexposed filly and she's an interesting contender upped to 1m2f on handicap debut

runners.name: Tai Hang Pegasus
runners.spotlight: Promising third on Yarmouth debut (1m1f, good to soft) last September but he's been well held in four subsequent runs including three on dirt at Meydan during the winter; now goes handicapping after a gelding operation but he needs a transformation back on turf.
runners.comment: H'cap newcomer but he needs a transformation back on turf on his return; has been gelded

races.race_name: Sandy Is Going The Distance At 80 Classified Stakes (Div I)

runners.name: Myna
runners.spotlight: Scored on AW at Lingfield in January, and got off the mark on turf at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm) last month; in decent nick at present and should go close.
runners.comment: Scored on AW at Lingfield in January and got off the mark on turf at Leicester last month.

runners.name: Belle Of Kt
runners.spotlight: Has been placed on her last five starts, all on AW, and got off the mark on the fourth of those in a similar event at Wolverhampton (1m4f) in March; drops back in trip on her turf debut but is in form and should not be far away.
runners.comment: Got off the mark in a similar event at Wolverhampton in March; turf debut.

runners.name: Pure Theory
runners.spotlight: 0-18 and she beat just one home at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm) last time; plenty to prove.
runners.comment: 0-18 and she beat just one home at Leicester last time.

runners.name: Summer Evening
runners.spotlight: Exposed maiden (6f-1m4f) who's been given a ten-week break since running as well as could have been expected in novice company at Bath (1m2f, good); withdrawn at Chepstow on Monday when the jockey got injured at the start; takes drop in class and may not be far away.
runners.comment: Exposed maiden but takes a drop in class and may not be far away.

runners.name: Qaaeadd
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden with the odd piece of modest form (good to firm and AW) but below that level on last two runs back on turf.
runners.comment: Ten-race maiden with the odd piece of modest form.

runners.name: Henfield
runners.spotlight: 0-4 but was beaten less than 5l at Windsor (1m, good to firm) last month on stable debut; still unexposed and steps up in trip; one to keep an eye on.
runners.comment: 0-4 but was beaten less than 5l at Windsor last month on stable debut.

runners.name: Shifra
runners.spotlight: 0-8 but produced her first worthwhile piece of form when runner-up in a handicap over C&D (good to firm) 19 days ago; holds place claims at least if she can reproduce that effort.
runners.comment: 0-8 but produced her first worthwhile piece of form when runner-up over C&D 19 days ago.

runners.name: Ruiz
runners.spotlight: Has yet to finish ahead of a rival in four starts; steps up in trip on turf debut but easy to dismiss.
runners.comment: Has yet to finish ahead of a rival in four starts; steps up in trip but easy to dismiss.

runners.name: High Boltage
runners.spotlight: Well held in all of his five starts; sports first-time tongue-tie on his first run in a classified and needs to show more.
runners.comment: Sports first-time tongue-tie on his first run in a classified and needs to show more.

runners.name: Pureis King
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner but well beaten in two 1m races on the Kempton AW and in an Epsom novice in April; takes a drop in class and not one to give up on; any market move may prove significant.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two 1m races on the Kempton AW and in an Epsom novice in April.

runners.name: Harry Don't Bite
runners.spotlight: Has shown little in six starts so far, latest when last of ten at Yarmouth (1m, soft) eight days ago on turf debut; steps up in trip but hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Last of ten at Yarmouth eight days ago on turf debut.

runners.name: Dubai Immo
runners.spotlight: All three wins have been on AW; hasn't made the frame in the last 12 months and was last of 12 when last seen at Kempton (1m4f, AW) in April; needs to show more.
runners.comment: Hasn't made the frame in the last 12 months and finished last when last seen in April.

races.race_name: Sandy Is Going The Distance At 80 Classified Stakes (Div II)

runners.name: Monks Mead
runners.spotlight: Doesn't win very often (3-36) but has two wins at this track, the latest a similar event (1m2f, AW) in January; has run creditably since until taking a dip when eighth of 15 at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) last month on his return to turf; has the form to run well here if putting his best foot forward.
runners.comment: Has two wins at this track but below form at Windsor last month.

runners.name: Rockafeller Skank
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a horse home in three starts last year; gelded since and has been a completely different proposition since returning this month, winning two classified events at Yarmouth (1m/1m2f, soft) and one at Wolverhampton AW on Tuesday night; looks open to more progress and he has leading claims.
runners.comment: Returned this month, winning two Yarmouth classifieds and another Wolverhampton Tuesday.

runners.name: Pablo Prince
runners.spotlight: All four wins have been on AW, including three at this track; has been running creditably this year and shouldn't be far away on this return to turf.
runners.comment: All four wins have been on AW, including three at this track.

runners.name: Moonlit Cloud
runners.spotlight: All four wins have been on turf but the last of those was over two years ago; two creditable efforts at Wolverhampton in April and Windsor in May but finished last of nine at Bath (1m2f, good; 4-1) 13 days ago; dips her toe into a classified for the first time, and at her best would be too good for these but has a question mark hanging over her at present.
runners.comment: All four wins have been on turf but the last of those was over two years ago.

runners.name: Hawaiian King
runners.spotlight: 0-10; tailed off on his turf debut at Bath in April and beaten over 15l at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) last month; cheekpieces back on replacing blinkers; needs to show more.
runners.comment: 0-10 and well beaten on his two outings on turf latest.
runners.owner: Aura Racing, Dave Brown, Joe Lane

runners.name: Pam's Sonnet
runners.spotlight: Well held in all five starts this year; steps up in trip and much more needed.
runners.comment: Well held in all five starts this year; steps up in trip and much more needed.

runners.name: Lady Aiyana
runners.spotlight: Showed modest form when trained by the Kublers last autumn but well held on her two runs for current yard; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Well held on her two runs for current yard; hard to fancy.

runners.name: Raise The Stakes
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way in five starts over 7f/1m on turf/AW; first-time visor replaces cheekpieces; needs to take a big step forward on this step up in trip.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way in five starts over 7f/1m on turf/AW.

runners.name: Eager Puccini
runners.spotlight: Yet to offer much in his eight runs to date; beaten 13l at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm) last month; wears first-time blinkers but not easy to warm to.
runners.comment: Yet to offer much in his eight runs to date; not easy to warm to.

runners.name: Amalfi Bluebell
runners.spotlight: Best effort last term was when seventh of nine in a maiden at this track (7f, good) in August; first run since September and best watched unless the market speaks in her favour.
runners.comment: First run since September and best watched unless the market speaks in her favour.

runners.name: Reel Power
runners.spotlight: Sole win came in a classified event at Brighton (1m, good to firm) in July 2023; fair fourth at Kempton in September but down the field in four starts since; needs to rediscover his form.
runners.comment: Fair fourth at Kempton in September but down the field in four starts since.

races.race_name: Gatwick Kia PV5 Restricted Novice Stakes (For Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Cape Fear
runners.spotlight: Placed in all four starts over 1m2f since returning in April, the latest when beaten under a length into second of six at Redcar (good to firm); consistent rather than progressive but, given he is bred to stay, this step up in trip may prove the key.
runners.comment: Placed in four starts over 1m2f since returning in April; step up in trip may prove key

runners.name: Al Maslool
runners.spotlight: Some promise in his first two starts on the AW, but hasn't progressed in three outings on turf; bred to be better than that, but still has questions to answer with cheekpieces replacing blinkers.
runners.comment: Some promise in first two starts on the AW, but hasn't progressed on turf; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Garrincha
runners.spotlight: 28-1 when second of 16 on his debut in a Listed bumper at Cheltenham on New Year's Day (race has produced a couple of winning hurdlers); dam won over 1m2f on the Flat and worth a second look.
runners.comment: Runner-up on debut in a Listed bumper at Cheltenham on New Year's Day; worth a second look

runners.name: Golden Step
runners.spotlight: Third in both starts at around this trip, but was beaten a long way on each occasion; others preferred.
runners.comment: Third in both starts, but was beaten a long way on each occasion; others preferred

runners.name: Moon Zabeel
runners.spotlight: 22,000gns yearling; half-brother to seven winners including Saint Baudolino (French 1m1f-1m3f including Group 2; RPR 119), Avilius (1m-1m4f including Australian Group 1; 114) and Well Of Wisdom (5f-1m including 2yo/Listed; 109); dam 1m turf 2yo/1m4f AW winner (104); impressive pedigree and any market support would be revealing.
runners.comment: Impressive pedigree and any market support would be revealing

runners.name: Argenteus
runners.spotlight: 25,000gns yearling, 50,000gns breeze-up 2yo; dam 1m-11.4f winner (including 2yo/Listed; RPR 109), half-sister to winners Distant Memories (1m2f Group 3) and Mohedian Lady (1m4f Listed), out of French 1m1f Listed winning half-sister to 1m4f Group 2 winner Ivan Luis; makes some appeal on breeding, but apprentice-ridden on debut and only considered if lively in the betting.
runners.comment: Some appeal on breeding, but apprentice-ridden on debut and only considered if backed
runners.owner: With Or Without You Partnership & Friend

runners.name: Joanna Hiffernan
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far into fourth of five on her second start at Windsor (11.4f, good to firm) last month, but more will be needed.
runners.comment: Not beaten far on her second start at Windsor last month, but more will be needed

races.race_name: Free Tips At chracingclub.com Handicap

runners.name: Rogue Empire
runners.spotlight: Showed the benefit of his reappearance third when getting off the mark over C&D (good) last month; followed up at Kempton (2m, AW) eight days ago in the manner of an improving sort; escapes a penalty for that win and is 8lb higher in future contests; the one to beat.
runners.comment: Off the mark over C&D last month; followed up at Kempton eight days ago; the one to beat.

runners.name: Kitty Foyle
runners.spotlight: Scored on the Flat in August 2024 but has mainly been campaigned over hurdles since, winning twice; not too badly treated and may well play her part.
runners.comment: Scored on the Flat in August 2024 but has mainly been campaigned over hurdles since.

runners.name: An Bradan Feasa
runners.spotlight: Won twice over hurdles (2m/2m4f, good) and twice on the Flat in 2025; looked as good as ever when second on the Flat over C&D (AW) last month, after a break, and is respected.
runners.comment: Looked as good as ever when second on the Flat over C&D last month.

runners.name: Naasma
runners.spotlight: Has six wins, the latest at Windsor (11.5f, good) last September off this mark; has run respectably since and has won over 2m but needs a true test to be fully effective.
runners.comment: Has six wins, the latest at Windsor last September off this mark.

runners.name: Diamond Bay
runners.spotlight: On a losing run and the switch to blinkers (retained) on his last two starts has not seen any upturn; others appeal more.
runners.comment: On a losing run and the switch to blinkers on his last two starts has not seen any upturn.

runners.name: Love Is Golden
runners.spotlight: Quite useful on the Flat up to 1m4f for the Johnstons and later won twice over hurdles for Gary Moore; always in rear on return from a lengthy absence on stable debut at Goodwood (1m4f, good to soft) 13 days ago; best watched for now.
runners.comment: Always in rear on return from a lengthy absence on stable debut at Goodwood 13 days ago.

races.race_name: Chris Haines Tips At Lingfield Handicap

runners.name: Bridget's Baby
runners.spotlight: Improved from her first three starts when beaten half a length into second of nine in a 0-50 classified event at Leicester (5f, good) ten days ago; although now in a handicap this looks even less competitive; non-runner at Ffos Las Wednesday.
runners.comment: Second in a 0-50 classified event at Leicester last time; this looks even less competitive

runners.name: Lesley Buckley
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in six starts and was making her turf/handicap debut when over 4l behind Ken Brulee here (6f, good) last time; 6lb better off and blinkers replace a visor, but still hard to warm to.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in six starts; blinkers replace a visor but still hard to warm to

runners.name: Ken Brulee
runners.spotlight: Improved plenty when making all over 6f here (good) last month, albeit the plum draw and first-time cheekpieces probably played their part; didn't back it up in a 0-50 classified event at Bath (5,7f, good) next time, but he is still a major player based on that penultimate effort.
runners.comment: Made all over 6f here last month; didn't back it up next time, but still a major player

runners.name: Kodi K
runners.spotlight: Hasn't finished within 14l of the winner in three starts over 6f/6.5f; improvement needed, but he has been found a weak race for his handicap debut; market may be helpful.
runners.comment: Improvement needed, but he has been found a weak race for his handicap debut

races.race_name: Free Bets On attheraces.com Restricted Novice Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Heddon Street
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to nine winners including My Lea (Italian 5f/6f including Group 3; RPR 106) and Royal Lea (Italian 5.5f/6f including Listed; 94); emphatic winner at Yarmouth (6f, good) in sole 2yo run but was only second of four on his Leicester reappearance (cheekpieces added; first run since gelded) last Monday; that was a bit disappointing but he ran into a useful prospect and he sets a clear standard here; key player.
runners.comment: Bit disappointing on reappearance last week but he sets a clear standard here; key player

runners.name: Eveninginparis
runners.spotlight: 26,000gns yearling; fifth foal; half-sister to winners Messerschmitt (1m 2yo, RPR 90; useful 2m hurdle) and Goose Rock (5f-1m2f; 67); dam 7f/7.4f 2yo winner (88); yard 16% in 3yo novice events this year and she needs a close look on debut.
runners.comment: Yard 16% in 3yo novice events this year and she needs a close look on debut

runners.name: Smooth Flight
runners.spotlight: 1,000gns yearling; gelded half-brother to five winners including Classic Flyer (5f-6f; RPR 77), Beat The Breeze (1m2f-1m6f; 74) and Tember (7f; 67); dam unraced; has speed and stamina in pedigree and market should guide on debut.
runners.comment: Half-brother to five winners and market should guide on debut

runners.name: Mariselle
runners.spotlight: 30,000euros breeze-up 2yo, 5,000gns 3yo; sixth foal; half-sister to three winners including Excelsius (7f AW 2yo/16.5f Flat, RPR 81; 2m/2m1f hurdle) and Andie One (US 8.5f turf/AW); dam French 7.7f-11.7f winner, half-sister to US 7f Grade 2 winner Dancing House; has good target to aim at on debut but she needs checking in market.
runners.comment: Has good target to aim at on debut but she needs checking in market

runners.name: Rumble Ruby
