runners.spotlight: Improved on her debut effort when 100-1 third at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) two weeks ago but was beaten almost 5l and that form is modest; remains best watched for now.
runners.comment: 100-1 third at Wolverhampton but was beaten almost 5l and that form is modest

runners.name: Moravian
runners.spotlight: Big prices and she's been beaten 10l in two runs (7f/6f) this spring, with an RPR of 41 on both occasions; passed over.
runners.comment: Big prices and she's been beaten 10l in two runs (7f/6f) this spring; passed over

runners.name: Ocean Force
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at big prices in all three runs, most recently when a remote fifth in a 7f novice here (good) last month; has a lot to find and he needs a transformation on this drop to 6f; one of two runners for yard.
runners.comment: Well beaten at big prices in all three runs and he has a lot to find dropped in trip

runners.name: Muchacho
runners.spotlight: Outsider and he was beaten 7l when last of four on recent Leicester debut (6f, good); finished behind Heddon Street in that race and he's probably one for further down the line.
runners.comment: Outsider and he was beaten 7l when last of four on recent Leicester debut (6f, good)

runners.name: Cortado Girl
runners.spotlight: 125-1 on debut here (7f, AW) in September and was always in rear after a slow start; hood is now added on this drop in trip; refused to enter stalls and withdrawn at Nottingham in April (300-1 at the time).
runners.comment: 125-1 on debut here (7f, AW) in September and was always in rear after a slow start

races.race_name: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Classified Stakes

runners.name: Dr Ali
runners.spotlight: Finished down the field on Tapeta in his last two starts but he won a 6f handicap at Wolverhampton in March; has possibilities if he can recapture that form but this looks tough from a low draw and he's raced almost exclusively on AW since last January.
runners.comment: Has raced almost exclusively on AW since last January and this looks tough from a low draw

runners.name: Charging Bull
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden but he produced a strong finish from off the pace to go close in a Nottingham handicap (5f, soft) last Thursday; has different conditions to deal with this time but he's unexposed on turf and is a big player if he can repeat that latest form; hood is removed.
runners.comment: Finished well to go close at Nottingham (5f) and he's a big player if he can back that up

runners.name: Magnificent Mel
runners.spotlight: 0-12 but she's finished runner-up in three of her last five starts including in a classified event at Bath (5.7f, good) last time; stall one could pose some problems here but she usually comes from off the pace and is not ruled out.
runners.comment: 0-12 but was runner-up in a classified event at Bath last time; in the mix

runners.name: Dios De La Guerra
runners.spotlight: Regressive maiden who has had breaks between his four handicap runs (7f/1m) and he's struggled in all of them; now drops into a classified event but he has a lot to prove on this switch to sprinting; hood worn last time is removed.
runners.comment: Regressive maiden who has struggled in his four handicaps; has lots to prove dropped to 6f

runners.name: Apple's Angel
runners.spotlight: Triple AW winner (5f/6f) but she's 0-8 on turf and was last of nine at Brighton (5.3f, good to firm) on her recent stable debut; that was after nine months off but she's struggled in her last three runs and others are more convincing.
runners.comment: 0-8 on turf and she was last of nine at Brighton on recent stable debut
runners.owner: P And Mrs L Rowe And John Turner

runners.name: Tass
runners.spotlight: Gelded son of Tasleet; well held at big prices in three novice/maiden events but he now drops into a 0-50 and is a possible improver on this drop back in trip; needs watching in the market.
runners.comment: Unexposed 3yo and he's a possible improver dropped into a classified event; market useful

runners.name: Lion Ring
runners.spotlight: Seven-time AW winner who was a creditable third in classified event at Wolverhampton (5f) two weeks ago; this step back up in trip is no problem but he's 0-17 on turf and was tailed off at Doncaster on his penultimate run.
runners.comment: Seven-time AW winner but he's 0-17 on turf and others are preferred

runners.name: Top Biller
runners.spotlight: Has a patchy profile and was out the back at Brighton last time but he finished runner-up in a Yarmouth handicap (6f, good) on his penultimate run; has claims if he can recapture that form and his last win was over C&D.
runners.comment: Hard to predict but last win was over C&D and he's a player if he can bounce back again

runners.name: Comedian Leader
runners.spotlight: Better known as a 7f/1m performer but her latest win (last August) was gained over C&D (good); predictably found 5f too sharp on AW here in March but finished well and wasn't beaten far; back up in trip today and has possibilities if she gets a good pace and some luck.
runners.comment: Better known as a 7f/1m performer but has claims if she gets a good pace and some luck

runners.name: Little Miss Magic
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 29 starts was at Bath (5f, good to firm) in August 2024; ran respectably in a classified event there (5.7f) two weeks ago but this looks tough on the figures and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Sole win was in August 2024 and this looks tough on the figures; opposable

runners.name: Voodoo Ray
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick in the spring of 2024 but he's drawn a blank since then and has been beaten 6l or more in his last five runs; opposable.
runners.comment: On long losing run and he's been beaten 6l or more in his last five runs

races.race_name: VIP Enhancement Packages At Southwell Racecourse Classified Stakes

runners.name: Sir Benedict
runners.spotlight: Multiple 5f/6f winner; has made a promising start to life with Ben Haslam this season, set a lot to do when fourth of nine in 5.5f Wetherby handicap two weeks ago; drops back into classified company now and this C&D scorer merits serious consideration.
runners.comment: Promising start for new yard, Wetherby fourth latest; C&D scorer rates a big player

runners.name: Uncle Sam
runners.spotlight: Placed four times at 6f/7f for David Thompson in 2025 but cheekpieces tried when only 11th in 6f Newcastle handicap in December; has gone well fresh so not out of things for new yard.
runners.comment: Placed four times at 6f/7f for David Thompson in 2025; not out of things for new yard

runners.name: Havin A Flyer
runners.spotlight: Yet to register a victory but comes here in decent nick, cheekpieces on when third of six in 5f Catterick handicap a week ago; not ruled out in her bid to score at the 19th attempt.
runners.comment: Respectable third at Catterick week ago; not ruled out in bid to score at the 19th attempt

runners.name: Bust A Moon
runners.spotlight: A fair 5f winner at his best but he ended his time with Ian McInnes in 2025 under a cloud and failed to beat a rival for his new yard in 5f Wolverhampton classified event 17 days ago.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival for his new yard in 5f Wolverhampton classified event 17 days ago

runners.name: Blueaway
runners.spotlight: Offered little in four outings for Rod Millman and she has failed to beat a rival in a pair of handicaps at Yarmouth and Lingfield for her new handler this spring; cheekpieces are reached for.
runners.comment: Failed to beat both runs for new handler this spring; cheekpieces are reached for

runners.name: Desert Champion
runners.spotlight: On a losing run but he shaped better than his placing suggests in 5f handicaps at Redcar and Lingfield on his first two runs this term; went without headgear when only sixth of eight in 5f Newcastle handicap last time but no surprise to see him play a prominent role here in refitted blinkers.
runners.comment: Without headgear when sixth at Newcastle but can bounce back here in refitted blinkers

runners.name: Top Star
runners.spotlight: Remains winless and he comes here below par, just fifth of eight in 6f Yarmouth handicap a week ago; more is required in refitted cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Remains winless and he comes here below par; more is required in refitted cheekpieces

runners.name: Highfield Jewel
runners.spotlight: Hinted at promise on her first two starts but there's been little to enthuse about since, last of nine in 5.5f Wetherby handicap two weeks ago; it's easy to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Last of nine in 5.5f Wetherby handicap two weeks ago; it's easy to look elsewhere

runners.name: Posh Maisie
runners.spotlight: Is 0-18 and she failed to beat a rival in 6f classified event at Nottingham a week ago; very hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: Is 0-18 and she failed to beat a rival in 6f classified event at Nottingham a week ago

runners.name: Dottie Diamond
runners.spotlight: Looks a poor maiden but she wasn't discredited after 19 months off with fourth of nine in 5f classified event at Leicester ten days ago; needs to build on it on her first go on Tapeta.
runners.comment: Not discredited after 19 months off with fourth at Leicester latest; needs to build on it

runners.name: Drish Samphire
runners.spotlight: Offered little in her six runs in Ireland for Donal Commins, off since coming in last of eight in 6f Dundalk handicap 17 months ago; starts out for a new yard with her fitness to prove.
runners.comment: Off since last at Dundalk 17 months ago; starts out for new yard with fitness to prove

races.race_name: Ladies Day Sunday 16th August Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Startling
runners.spotlight: A fair and reliable 5f AW winner for David O'Meara in 2025; comes here on the back of a solid third of 11 for her new stable in 5f Thirsk handicap 13 days ago so can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Solid third for her new stable in 5f Thirsk handicap 13 days ago, so can't be ruled out

runners.name: Life After Love
runners.spotlight: Has thrived in 2026 and she notched a third C&D victory in April; posted another good effort on her turf debut when third of eight at Lingfield (5f) last month; can race off a 1lb lower mark so she holds leading claims back here.
runners.comment: Notched third C&D win in April; good turf third at Lingfield latest; leading claims

runners.name: La Belle Forest
runners.spotlight: Landed a maiden over C&D in March for Karl Burke but well below par both subsequent runs in 5f handicaps, only sixth of eight for her new yard at Ffos Las two weeks ago; has something to prove.
runners.comment: Well below par last two runs, sixth for new yard at Ffos Las two weeks ago

runners.name: Sarafina Mshairi
runners.spotlight: Signed off for 2025 with 6f success here in October; looked rusty after seven months off when ninth of ten in 5f Windsor handicap last month; should strip sharper here.
runners.comment: Course winner; rusty after seven months off when ninth at Windsor; should strip sharper

runners.name: J Street
runners.spotlight: Ended her time with Ivan Furtado with a very good 5f Wolverhampton second in October; yet to hit that form for her current yard but not disgraced with fifth of 11 in 5f Thirsk handicap 13 days ago; possibilities off an easing mark.
runners.comment: Yet to hit top form for current yard but a fair Thirsk fifth latest; possibilities

runners.name: Solid Bond
runners.spotlight: Yet to register a victory and she underwent breathing surgery before coming in only eighth of 11 in 5f Thirsk handicap 13 days ago; needs this switch to Tapeta to spark major progress.
runners.comment: Had wind op before eighth at Thirsk latest; needs switch to Tapeta to spark progress

runners.name: Fantasy Obsessor
runners.spotlight: Course winner who has bounced back to form since the cheekpieces went on, runner-up over C&D four weeks ago; she must enter calculations again off the same mark.
runners.comment: Course winner; good runner-up over C&D four weeks ago; must enter calculations again

runners.name: Torfrida
runners.spotlight: Winless since 2023 and she's been off the track since beating one in 5f Pontefract handicap 11 months ago; needs to hit the ground running on her Tapeta debut.
runners.comment: Off since beating one at Pontefract 11 months ago; needs to hit the ground running

races.race_name: Lionel Henry James Memorial Handicap

runners.name: Spirit Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Hinted at promise on his first two runs in 1m4f maidens at Salisbury and Haydock before being well held in the face of a stiff task in Yarmouth match three weeks ago; could still do better on his handicap debut with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Hinted at promise but stiff task in Yarmouth match; could still do better on h'cap debut

runners.name: Project Kinsman
runners.spotlight: Placed in a pair of 1m2f Newcastle handicaps this spring before a below-par eighth in classified event there when tried in cheekpieces; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Cheekpieces on when below-par eighth in Newcastle classified event; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Dalamara
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with 1m nursery success at Newcastle in December; in the frame all three runs this season, not enjoying the smoothest of passages when a solid fourth of seven in 1m2f Wetherby handicap two weeks ago; not out of things stepping up in trip.
runners.comment: In the frame all three runs this season, solid Wetherby fourth latest; not out of things

runners.name: Manbearpig
runners.spotlight: Modest form at 2yrs; gelded/off five months before returning with a fair fifth of 11 on his handicap debut at Thirsk (1m4f) last month; needs to build on it.
runners.comment: Gelded/off five months before fair fifth at Thirsk last month; needs to build on it

runners.name: Echo Valley
runners.spotlight: Only poor form shown in her five outings to date, though not discredited with fourth of eight in 1m2f Chepstow handicap 20 days ago; more is required on her first go over 1m4f.
runners.comment: Not discredited with fourth at Chepstow 20 days ago; more required on first go over 1m4f

runners.name: Mountbatten
runners.spotlight: Son of Japan who has been brought along steadily, a patiently-ridden seventh of 12 in 1m AW Lingfield novice when last seen in December; appeals as an improver now handicapping stepped up markedly in trip; interesting.
runners.comment: Brought along steadily, Lingfield seventh latest; likely improver now h'capping up in trip

races.race_name: Southwell Racecourse Conferences And Events Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Slalom
runners.spotlight: 8-1, made a promising start when third of ten in 7f Kempton novice 22 days ago, staying on late having run green; this son of No Nay Never has more to offer, especially over 1m.
runners.comment: Promising start with staying-on Kempton third; he has more to offer, especially over 1m

runners.name: Sheikhnshah
runners.spotlight: Son of Persian King who has shaped well on both his runs, hooded and still looking green when third of ten in 1m Redcar novice last month; open to more progress.
runners.comment: Hooded and still looked green when third in Redcar novice last month; open to progress

runners.name: Centigrade
runners.spotlight: Twice-raced Too Darn Hot colt who looked an excellent prospect when easily making all in 1m Newbury novice in October 2024; absent since but he remains with considerable potential.
runners.comment: Easily made all at Newbury in 2024; absent since but remains with considerable potential

runners.name: Cliff Danger
runners.spotlight: Bated Breath gelding who built on debut sixth when fourth of ten in 1m2f Nottingham novice last month; he can do better still, especially once qualified for handicaps.
runners.comment: Fourth at Nottingham last month; can do better still, especially when h'capping

runners.name: Golden Buddy
runners.spotlight: Never dangerous in a pair of maidens over C&D and at Wolverhampton this summer; he can do better in the longer term for leading stable.
runners.comment: Never dangerous in maidens over C&D and Wolverhampton; can do better in the longer term

runners.name: Burning Impact
runners.spotlight: 155,000 guineas yearling; Too Darn Hot colt; half-brother to three winners, notably Program Trading (US 8.5f-9.5f Grade 1; RPR 121); dam, 1m AW 2yo winner (RPR 88), is a half-sister to 14.5f Group 2 winner Silk Sari; appeals on paper but yard also have Centigrade in here; betting should prove informative.
runners.comment: Too Darn Hot colt; appeals on paper but yard have Centigrade in here; betting informative

runners.name: Star Of The Desert
runners.spotlight: $40,000 foal, $6,000 yearling, 100,000euros 2yo, 2,000gns 3yo; Knicks Go gelding; half-brother to US 6f dirt winner Moon Rose; dam, 6f dirt winner, is a half-sister to 1m Grade 3 winner Super Freaky, out of half-sister to Kentucky Derby/Preakness/Dubai World Cup winner Silver Charm; the betting can guide for this newcomer.
runners.comment: Knicks Go gelding; the betting can guide for this newcomer

runners.name: Clatford
runners.spotlight: Improved from his debut with second of nine in C&D novice in March; this demands another step forward though.
runners.comment: Improved from debut with second of nine in C&D novice in March; this demands more though

runners.name: Moral Victory
runners.spotlight: 42,000gns foal; Bated Breath gelding; half-brother to 1m7f hurdle winner Newport; unraced dam closely related to 6f Group 3 winner The Happy Prince and 1m Listed winner Ziegfeld; unraced 5yo; the betting can be an indicator on his first run.
runners.comment: Unraced 5yo; the betting can be an indicator on his first run

runners.name: Alkaios
runners.spotlight: Failed to build on debut fifth here when last of nine in 11.5f Haydock maiden four weeks ago; hood is reached for now.
runners.comment: Last of nine in 11.5f Haydock maiden four weeks ago; hood is reached for now

runners.name: Spectical
runners.spotlight: New Bay colt; shaped with promise on both runs for Jessica Harrington last autumn, still looking green when fourth of 13 in 7f Listowel maiden on latter occasion; more to come for his new yard.
runners.comment: Shaped with promise both runs for Jessica Harrington at 2yrs; more to offer for new yard

runners.name: Keble Forever
runners.spotlight: Too Darn Hot colt; dam 1m-1m4f winner (Group 2 Lancashire Oaks; RPR 109); makes his belated debut but in top hands and well worth considering, especially if the market vibes are positive.
runners.comment: Belated debut but well worth considering, especially if the market vibes are positive

runners.name: Halimede
runners.spotlight: Hooded and well held in maidens over C&D and at Wolverhampton this summer; one for the longer term.
runners.comment: Hooded and well held in maidens over C&D and Wolverhampton; one for the longer term

runners.name: Burrows Silver
runners.spotlight: 50-1, last of ten in maiden at Thirsk (7f, good) on her debut 13 days ago; lots more is required.
runners.comment: Last of ten in maiden at Thirsk on her debut 13 days ago; lots more is required
runners.owner: Mrs C Casterton & Mrs J Morgan

races.race_name: Monday 31st August Family Fun Day Confined Handicap (No Win Since The 2025 Flat Turf Season)

runners.name: Silver Trumpet
runners.spotlight: Three encouraging runs this year, all in a higher grade than this; C&D winner; interesting contender back at this level.
runners.comment: Three encouraging runs in stronger company this year; one to consider

runners.name: Spirit Of Albion
runners.spotlight: The odd promising effort for Amanda Perrett, including on AW (Polytrack); dropped right away on his stable/seasonal debut last month (7f, good) but a 5lb drop seems generous and a hood is now fitted; too soon to write off and he's a contender despite his wide stall.
runners.comment: Flopped on stable/seasonal debut but dropped 5lb and now tried in a hood; interesting

runners.name: Star Of Mali
runners.spotlight: C&D win last October was a convincing one and he is only 2lb higher today; solid third at Wolverhampton when last seen in January; still has potential after just nine starts.
runners.comment: C&D winner last October; not fully exposed after nine starts; big player back from a break

runners.name: Judicature
runners.spotlight: Beaten in seven handicaps since his 6f novice win at Newcastle last August; went close at Carlisle (7f, soft) last month but he failed to build on it at Beverley nine days later.
runners.comment: Yet to win a handicap but went close on soft turf last month; backward step latest

runners.name: Blazing Son
runners.spotlight: 4lb lower than for his win at Newcastle (7f) last September; up and down in 2026 and others bring stronger claims.
runners.comment: Mixed bag in 2026; should be well treated but others bring stronger claims

runners.name: Cy Twombly
runners.spotlight: Signs of ability for the Crisfords in the autumn of 2024; tailed off on handicap debut in January and again well beaten in a Wolverhampton maiden 17 days ago; down in the weights but risky.
runners.comment: Modest form for current yard and hard to recommend despite a reduced mark

runners.name: Ribenska
runners.spotlight: Her only win came at odds of 1-16 in a three-runner fillies' maiden at Lingfield (7f, AW) last September; ran well for second over C&D on her reappearance but not in the same form at Kempton six weeks ago.
runners.comment: Good C&D run in April but she failed to back it up at Kempton six weeks ago

runners.name: Reds And Wolves
runners.spotlight: Three runs in the first half of 2025 were encouraging; gelded prior to this seasonal/handicap debut; still has potential and the betting should be revealing after 384 days off; tongue-tie returns.
runners.comment: Promise in three 3yo runs; absent 384 days but brings potential to this handicap debut

runners.name: Macanudo
runners.spotlight: Just one win to his name after 26 attempts; dropped 5lb during his 426-day absence but others still bring more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Poor strike-rate and is returning from 426 days off; not a solid option
runners.owner: Giddy Up You Good Thing Racing Syndicate

runners.name: Alfa Whiteburd
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on peak form and his midfield finish at Doncaster (7f, good to soft) in March, on first run after wind surgery, was encouraging; he has a higher mark to contend with back on AW but this represents a drop in class and he shouldn't be underestimated.
runners.comment: Return to AW is a plus and he is on a dangerous mark; latest Doncaster run was encouraging

races.race_name: Book The Bistro In Seasons Restaurant Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Town Queen
runners.spotlight: Blue Point filly; fair form when runner-up on all her three starts this season, beaten by a next-time  winner in 7f Chester maiden last time; holds very good form claims on her Tapeta debut.
runners.comment: Second all three runs, beaten by next-time winner at Chester latest; very good claims

runners.name: Planet Seeker
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start in 6f Goodwood maiden in August and still looked inexperienced when staying on late into third in Group 3 event at Salisbury (6f) the following month; returns from nine months off with more to come; big shout.
runners.comment: Winning start at Goodwood before third in Group 3 at Salisbury; more to come so big shout

runners.name: Name The Day
runners.spotlight: 18-1, encouraging start when fourth of ten in novice at Windsor (6f, good to firm) 17 days ago, staying on late under a patient ride; seems sure to take a step forward.
runners.comment: Encouraging debut fourth at Windsor; seems sure to take a step forward

runners.name: Star Trail
runners.spotlight: 155,000euros yearling; Oasis Dream filly; half-sister to four winners, notably Off Trail (Japanese 7f-1m1f Grade 2; RPR 118) and Rosa Imperial (5f-1m Group 3; RPR 112); dam unraced half-sister to Australian 12.5f Group 1 winner; appeals on paper but has had wind surgery ahead of her debut; the market should prove a good guide.
runners.comment: Appeals on paper but has had wind surgery; market should prove a good guide

runners.name: Star Velocity
runners.spotlight: 80,000gns yearling; Zoustar filly; sister to 5f AW winner Sweet Carolina (RPR 74); dam 7f-8.6f winner (AW Listed; RPR 103); yard also has Planet Seeker in here but this newcomer needs considering.
runners.comment: Zoustar filly; yard also has Planet Seeker in here but this newcomer needs considering

runners.name: Veil Of Clouds
runners.spotlight: Sent off 5-4 favourite for her Wetherby debut (5.5f, good to firm) two weeks ago but she could never land a blow when fifth of six; better can be expected now stepping up in trip.
runners.comment: 5-4 but fifth of six on Wetherby debut; more to come now stepping up in trip

runners.name: Gardening
runners.spotlight: Has cut little ice in a pair of maidens at Newcastle (for Karl Burke) in November and Catterick last month; hard to warm to.
runners.comment: Has cut little ice in maidens at Newcastle (for Karl Burke) in November and Catterick

runners.name: Mass For Kathleen
runners.spotlight: Failed to build on Haydock debut third when tenth of 11 in 7f Doncaster novice 12 days ago; needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Failed to build on Haydock debut third when tenth of 11 in 7f Doncaster novice 12 days ago

runners.name: Material Girl
runners.spotlight: 16-1 on debut when seventh of ten in novice at Windsor (6f, good to firm) 17 days ago; much more is required.
runners.comment: Debut seventh of ten at Windsor (16-1); much more is required

runners.name: Mimime
runners.spotlight: 18-1, never-dangerous seventh of ten in maiden at Leicester (6f, good to firm) on her first run 24 days ago; significantly more is required.
runners.comment: Seventh of ten at Leicester on her first run 24 days ago; significantly more is required

runners.name: Park Lane Penny
runners.spotlight: Well held in 1m Redcar maiden and 7f Doncaster novice this summer; she's hard to warm to.
runners.comment: Well held in 1m Redcar maiden and 7f Doncaster novice this summer; she's hard to warm to

races.race_name: Golf And Gallop Southwell Golf Club Handicap

runners.name: Bone Marra
runners.spotlight: Dual scorer (5f/7f) at Wolverhampton as 2yo; advanced his form when runner-up in 6f Lingfield handicap on return but never able to challenge in 7f Goodwood handicap since; no surprise to see him bounce back returned to AW.
runners.comment: Good Lingfield second but never able to challenge at Goodwood since; can bounce back here

runners.name: Sanaam
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with 7f maiden success here in December but yet to fire in 7f handicaps at Wetherby and Doncaster this season; returns to AW with cheekpieces fitted and something to prove.
runners.comment: Yet to fire in pair of turf handicaps this season; returns to AW with cheekpieces fitted

runners.name: Up The Agenda
runners.spotlight: Scored three times at 6f on the AW over the winter; off for four months before a never-dangerous seventh of 14 in 6f Windsor handicap 26 days ago and should be sharper here.
runners.comment: Off four months before seventh of 14 at Windsor 26 days ao and should strip fitter here

runners.name: Ay Up Duck
runners.spotlight: Gained a first win at the third attempt in 7f maiden here in April but has been well held in handicaps at Ascot (7f) and Leicester (6f) since; needs this return to Tapeta to spark a revival.
runners.comment: Won here in April but well held in handicaps at Ascot (7f) and Leicester (6f) since

runners.name: Lady Kodiac
runners.spotlight: Dual 6f AW novice winner as 2yo; in good form in handicaps this season but she seemed not to last home over 7f when sixth of nine at Carlisle 19 days ago; can make her presence felt reverting to 6f.
runners.comment: Seemed not to stay 7f when sixth at Carlisle latest; can make presence felt back at 6f

runners.name: Blue Orbit
runners.spotlight: A fair 5f juvenile winner; returned with good efforts to make the frame in 5f handicaps at Wolverhampton and Bath this spring; found big-field Ascot handicap (6f) last time too tough an ask but can get back on track here.
runners.comment: Never able to challenge in big-field Ascot handicap (6f) last time; can get back on track

runners.name: Enricher
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with 6f Wolverhampton AW maiden victory in August but off for ten months before finishing only tenth of 12 in 6f Leicester handicap last month; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Off ten months before tenth in 6f Leicester handicap last month; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Ghisa
runners.spotlight: Won her first two starts (including here) for Ollie Pears as 2yo; performed with credit over the winter for current yard in Meydan so not out of things on her handicap debut.
runners.comment: Course winner; ran with credit for current yard at Meydan in winter so not out of things

runners.name: Gaurdman
runners.spotlight: Progressive 2yo who signed off his first season with victory in 7f Haydock novice in September, just holding on having gone clear 1f out; he looks to have more to offer back in trip for his handicap debut; major shout with yard firmly among the winners.
runners.comment: Just held on in Haydock novice in September; major player back in trip on handicap debut

races.race_name: Singapore Pools Prix de la Porte Maillot (Group 3) (Turf)

runners.name: Silius
runners.spotlight: Fairly dependable type in Group company over this trip and a mile, with him gaining a career-best this year when landing a mile Listed event at Saint-Cloud in March on very soft ground; sound effort in the C&D Prix du Palais-Royal 28 days ago and should be involved in the finish.
runners.comment: Fairly dependable type in Group company over this trip and a mile; should be involved

runners.name: Synaran
runners.spotlight: Supplemented for this; showed useful form as a 2yo but done better this season following a gelding operation, winning a C&D Listed event on good to soft at April before running to a similar level when third in the Group 2 Prix Paul de Moussac, again over C&D just 11 days ago; open to plenty of improvement so should be involved.
runners.comment: Makes a quick reappearance after good run over C&D; has more to come

runners.name: Khovikhov
runners.spotlight: Capable of decent form but not always consistent and was comfortably held as an 82-1 shot in the Poule d'Essai Des Poulain when last seen; drop in trip and class may help but no doubt needs to up his game; Alexis Pouchin rides for first time.
runners.comment: Not always consistent but drop in trip and class may help

runners.name: Dark Trooper
runners.spotlight: Just over 2l behind King Gold in this last year when seventh but, unlike that rival, progressed after and was in great heart at Doha and Abu Dhabi during the winter, with his last piece of form over a mile being franked since; won't mind the drop in trip and looks a leading player.
runners.comment: Progressed since 7th in this 12 months ago and last piece of form has been boosed since

runners.name: Dino
runners.spotlight: Strung together a winning sequence in the Czech Republic and Slovakia during 2024/2025 and held his own at Listed level on his last three starts, most recently when landing one over 6.5f at Hanover on good ground; knows how to get his head in front so not dismissed for a place.
runners.comment: Knows how to win and won a 6.5 Listed event last time; not dismissed for a place

runners.name: No Limit Dream
runners.spotlight: Capable performer at this trip in handicaps but would need a clear career-best effort to land this judged on his last two runs, which were over this C&D; first Group-race start on his 46th outing.
runners.comment: Capable in handicaps but very hard to fancy in this company.
runners.owner: Mathieu Andria, Cyril Cois Et Al

runners.name: Lanzelot Gold
runners.spotlight: Won a valuable C&D handicap in April on good to soft but then went on to finish over 3l behind Fort Payne in a conditions race here in the same month and was behind Silius in a Group 3 after that before a more modest performance in a German Listed race; work to do to in current form.
runners.comment: Only wins have come in handicaps and a maiden, and work to do in current form

runners.name: King Gold
runners.spotlight: Trying to land this race for a fourth successive year; hasn't performed anywhere near the level he produced in this 12 months ago since, albeit a couple were at a higher grade; clearly likes this C&D, though, and sure to have been primed for a repeat bid; Aurelien Lemaitre hasn't ridden him since 2020.
runners.comment: Looking to win this race for a fourth year straight, so sure to be primed for this

runners.name: Fort Payne
runners.spotlight: Smart sort at his best and bounced back from some underwhelming performances in Meydan to land a C&D conditions event when last seen 53 days ago; a repeat of that level would see him a player here but does face some less-exposed types; Clement Lecoeuvre on for first time.
runners.comment: Won a C&D conditions event when last seen 53 days ago, but needs more to land this
runners.owner: Alain Jathiere, Mme Philippe Demercastel & Nicolas Caullery

races.race_name: Prix de Thiberville (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)

runners.name: Pierian
runners.owner: Lord N P V J Rothschild

runners.name: Stealitwithakiss
runners.owner: Jennifer Bianco, Linda Shanahan & Emily Magnier
